checked.comment: Equinix-authored CH4 IBX technical spec (© 2015), mirrored on Cloudscene CDN. Fetched binary and extracted text. All four researcher quotes verbatim: address '350 East Cermak Road 8th Floor Chicago, IL 60616'; 'CH4 customers can access CH1 located on the 5th Floor and CH2 located on the 6th Floor within the same building'; 'Equinix uses Digital Realty Trust (DRT) for our facilities and environmental protection controls at CH4'; CME LNET / ICE gateway sentence. Also confirms 1,330 sq ft antenna platform shared with CH1. Primary operator doc, though dated 2015.

CME Group client systems wiki, last updated Dec 15, 2025. Verbatim: 'CME Co-location facilities at DC3 in Aurora, 350 East Cermak, Equinix NY4/NY5 in Secaucus and Equinix LD4/LD5 in Slough' (Cert/New Release platform access). Primary operator doc; supports current/active status.

Verbatim: 'Bulk Transport is the name given to a fibre optic connection back to the old matching and co-lo centre at 350 East Cermak Street in Chicago proper.' Aurora Globex DC opened August 2010. Credible specialist press = reported.

Verbatim: 'most financial firms usually host their servers in the Equinix CH1/4/5 facilities at 350 E Cermak.' Supports ongoing proximity-hosting role; article frames Cermak as the alternative to direct Aurora colocation.

Confirms 'This site is leased from Digital Realty Trust in the Digital Realty: ORD10 building' and gives CH1 3.3 / CH2 2.5 / CH4 2.4 = 8.2 MW. Industry database (weakest evidence tier); researcher's own notes admit cross-database variance 6.0–10.35 MW, so this alone cannot carry a hard power_mw value — figure demoted to attributed note.

McKay Brothers' own live media page. June 2, 2015 item: 'One-way latency between 350 E. Cermak and NY5 is 3.885 milliseconds' on their Illinois–New Jersey microwave network. Verifies Cermak–NY5 route, operator, medium. Primary operator doc.

3 March 2014. Verbatim: 'McKay has opened additional points of presence at data centres in Piscataway, New Jersey and at 350 E Cermak in Chicago'; Cermak clients access 'Carteret at 7.82 milliseconds' round trip via the microwave network. At publication Cermak was westbound-listen only, eastbound injection weeks away. Press = reported.
My own additional check, not a researcher URL — 404. McKay's live /media page and CME's Dec-2025 wiki already support current status, so no claim depends on this.
My own additional corroboration attempt — fetch timed out. Not a researcher URL; nothing depends on it.

results.reasoning: Adversarial re-check could not refute the core claims. All 7 researcher URLs are live and support what they are cited for: the Equinix-authored CH4 IBX spec PDF (fetched as binary via WebFetch, text extracted locally) contains every quoted phrase verbatim, including the full street address, CH1/CH2 floor placement, Digital Realty Trust attribution, and CME LNET / ICE gateways; CME's own client wiki (updated Dec 15, 2025) currently lists 350 East Cermak among CME co-location facilities; A-Team, Databento, Baxtel and McKay's media page all verify their respective quotes. Confidence 'confirmed' stands on two independent primary operator sources (Equinix spec + CME wiki). Coordinates (41.85338, -87.61861) sit on the 350 E Cermak block; 'exact' precision is justified by the source-stated full street address. Changes imposed: (1) power_mw 8.2 -> null — only verified source is Baxtel, a weakest-tier industry database, and the researcher's own notes concede figures range 6.0–10.35 MW across databases; the 8.2 MW figure survives only as an attributed caveat in capacity_note. (2) Stripped unverified specifics from capacity_note: the '~1.13M sq ft' building size and the datacenterHawk 10.35 MW figure (that URL was never fetched by anyone). (3) Noted the CH4 spec is a 2015 document; currency is carried by the Dec-2025 CME wiki and live Baxtel/Databento pages. Arcs: all three survive — route, operator and medium each verified by their evidence URL (McKay operator page = confirmed; two press-sourced arcs correctly graded reported); arc destinations T1-002/T1-003/T1-004 exist in data/sites_seed.csv and match (Carteret, Secaucus NY4/NY5, Aurora). Active status for the McKay arcs is supported by McKay's live media page and, indirectly, a PRNewswire Aurora–Cermak release found in search; the CME fiber arc's active status is supported by the Dec-2025 CME wiki listing. Verdict: promote_with_changes.
results.research_notes: Searches: Equinix CH1/CH2 '350 East Cermak' address; Digital Realty/ORD10 ownership; CME matching engine pre-Aurora history; McKay Brothers/Quincy Data Cermak POP. Fetched and verified: Equinix CH4 IBX+ technical spec PDF (hosted by Cloudscene — Equinix-authored, gives verbatim address '350 East Cermak Road, 8th Floor, Chicago, IL 60616', CH1=5th fl, CH2=6th fl, Digital Realty Trust facilities attribution, CME LNET / ICE gateways, shared rooftop antenna platform); CME Group client wiki (lists '350 East Cermak' among CME co-location facilities for Cert/New Release access); A-Team Insight CME Aurora quick-facts (calls Cermak 'the old matching and co-lo centre', Aurora Globex DC opened Aug 2010, 'Bulk Transport' fiber back to Cermak); Databento CME colocation blog (proximity hosting: 'most financial firms usually host their servers in the Equinix CH1/4/5 facilities at 350 E Cermak'); Baxtel (Digital Realty ORD10, floors, 8.2 MW combined Equinix power); A-Team McKay build-out (Cermak POP opened 2014) and mckay-brothers.com/media (Cermak–NY5 one-way 3.885 ms). Coordinates geocoded from the documented address via OSM Nominatim (41.8533827, -87.6186071). NOT findable/blocked: equinix.com pages and PDFs returned HTTP 403 to fetches (address instead sourced from the Equinix-authored PDF mirrored on Cloudscene); datacentermap.com returned 429; no primary/press source found stating an exact date CME's matching engine left Cermak (only Aurora opening Aug 2010, co-lo live Jan 2012); power figures for the Equinix floors vary across industry databases (6.0–10.35 MW depending on source and date) — 8.2 MW used with caveat; building-wide Digital Realty power not cited from a fetched primary source, so omitted.

Caveat: release describes McKay's 'wireless networks' without naming microwave for this segment, and 4.3 ms exceeds the ~2.5 ms lightspeed minimum, so segments may be wired; microwave is the operator's standard long-haul medium.

results.reasoning: All six researcher evidence URLs were fetched: all live and all support their claims, with one quote discrepancy. Verified verbatim: TMX operator page ('suburban location' sentence; no city named), TNS May 2023 release ('with TMX out of the Markham data center' — the key location source), TMX June 2010 completion release (200 co-location spaces; no city named), Inflect building page (3500 Steeles Avenue East Markham ON L3R 0X1; 150 W/sq ft), McKay Brothers /media page (Mahwah-Markham <1.754 ms, Jan 2024; CME-TMX Toronto <4.3 ms, Mar 2023; Feb 2026 TMX Markham/TR2 item), and the A-Team Insight article. The researcher's McKay quote ('Fastest private bandwidth between Mahwah and TMX Group in Markham, Ontario') was not returned by my fetch of /media, so I replaced it with a verified verbatim quote; the Inflect address quote was adjusted to the exact page rendering. Changes made: (1) stripped Options Technology from firms_linked — its only source (Businesswire 2019) is bot-walled (timed out twice for me as well as the researcher) and no accessible second source exists, so under project rules the claim drops; (2) added a NEW verified primary source discovered during checking, ICE Global Network's Toronto wireless page (ice.com), which as exchange documentation independently confirms TMX's main data centre houses TSX/TSX-V/Alpha/MX, that delivery is to Markham (MCC), and that the NJ-Markham corridor uses microwave; (3) swapped the 2010 release quote to the sentence that actually backs the 200-space capacity claim; (4) moved arc 2's evidence_url from the A-Team press article to the verified McKay operator /media page so its 'confirmed' grade rests on a primary source, with press kept as corroboration in the note; (5) flagged honestly in arc 2's note that the operator says 'wireless' rather than 'microwave' for the CME-Toronto segment and that the 4.3 ms latency leaves room for wired segments, whereas arc 1's 1.754 ms is at lightspeed and can only be radio. Confidence grades survive strictly: the record is 'confirmed' via multiple verified primary sources (operator docs + three corporate announcements + exchange docs); both arcs 'confirmed' via the operator's own announcements. Geography checks out (Markham, Canada per TNS/McKay/ICE/Inflect; coords plausible for 3500 Steeles Ave E, kept 'approximate' since the address is industry-database-sourced and the complex is large). power_mw stays null — the 1.8 MW figure surfaced only in an unfetchable datacenters.com snippet. Dead links (McKay/Quincy permalinks, Yahoo HK, 01net) were confirmed dead and excluded, matching the researcher's own exclusions. Arc origins T1-001 (NYSE Mahwah) and T1-004 (CyrusOne Aurora/CME) verified against data/sites_seed.csv. Verdict: promote_with_changes — the researcher's work was accurate and honestly caveated; corrections are the dropped Options linkage, one unverifiable quote replaced, a stronger evidence set, and a transparent medium caveat on the CME arc.
results.research_notes: Searches run: TMX colocation Markham/matching engine; TMX Datalinx co-location address; "3500 Steeles" TMX; Options Technology "TMX Markham"; TSX matching engine Markham; microwave Markham-NJ/Chicago HFT routes. Fetched and verified: TMX Datalinx co-location page (operator docs — confirms TMX-run suburban colo, markets/feeds, but publishes NO city or street address), TMX 2010 completion press release via newswire.ca (200 colo spaces; dateline Toronto, city of facility not named), TNS May 2023 press release (explicitly 'TMX out of the Markham data center'; also notes other Canadian venues — TradelogiQ, NEO, CSE, Nasdaq Canada, Cboe MATCHnow — sit in Equinix TR2 Toronto, a possible future execution-tier candidate not in scope here), inflect.com building page (address 3500 Steeles Avenue East, Markham, ON L3R 0X1; 150 W/sq ft), McKay Brothers media page (operator confirmation of Mahwah–Markham <1.754 ms and Aurora–TMX <4.3 ms wireless routes; Feb 2026 item: 'Fast wireless and wired connectivity...now link TMX Markham and Equinix TR2 with key US trading venues'), A-Team Insight article on the NJ–Toronto launch. NOT findable/verifiable in public record: any MW power figure for the site; any individually named quant/HFT tenant firms (only service providers TNS, Options Technology, McKay Brothers are public); TMX itself never publishes the street address. Fetch failures (excluded from evidence): Businesswire Options 2019 'TMX Markham (MCC)' release timed out twice (content seen only in search snippets); datacenters.com returned HTTP 429; mckay-brothers.com individual press-release permalinks and quincy-data.com permalink returned 404 (site restructure) — operator /media page used instead. Coordinates from OSM Nominatim geocode of the documented address (43.8177, -79.3394, building-level match 'Liberty Centre', former IBM complex, north/Markham side of Steeles Ave); graded approximate because the 549k sq ft four-tower complex spans a large footprint.

results.reasoning: Adversarial verification found the researcher's output substantially accurate: all 7 evidence URLs fetched live on 2026-07-05 and each contains the quoted text and supports its claim (B3 FAQ: verbatim address, PUMA co-housed, 9+6 engines, 10 MW, Tier III, LEED, 22 µs; B3 DC page: Santana de Parnaíba + XV de Novembro DR site founded 2008; B3 2017 notice: PUMA migration Nov 2017; Estado de Minas Feb 2026: active co-location and HFT share; Asset Servicing Times: TNS at B3 Nov 2023; Seaborn SeaSpeed page: B3 SPA to 1400 Federal Blvd Carteret on Seabras-1 at 105.08 ms; Seaborn–Anova PR 24 Oct 2019: fiber+microwave composite to CME). Arc destinations T1-002 (Nasdaq Carteret) and T1-004 (CME Aurora) match data/sites_seed.csv. Coordinates for record 1 independently confirmed via Nominatim (street resolves to -23.46637, -46.86569, CEP matching the B3 FAQ). Changes made: (1) the firms_linked clause 'PUMA co-developed with CME Group' had no supporting URL — sourced and verified via the PR Newswire release of the BM&FBOVESPA/CME announcement (the cmegroup.com original timed out) and added to evidence rather than stripped; (2) record 2's coord_precision downgraded from approximate to city_level because B3 publishes no address and the downtown placement is inferred from the facility name (B3's units page documenting Prédio XV at Rua XV de Novembro 275 added as placement basis, explicitly flagged as not labeling a data center); (3) arc 2 note amended to flag that 'fiber' covers only the subsea leg (NJ–Chicago leg is microwave per the source) and that the 2019 composite's current status is presumed from the still-live subsea product page. Confidence grades stand: record 1 confirmed (exchange primary docs), record 2 confirmed for existence/role with location uncertainty carried in coord_precision, both arcs confirmed (operator docs/corporate announcement). Nothing needed rejection; no unsupported capacity figures, power values, or firm linkages remain.
results.research_notes: SEARCHED: B3 official co-location pages (EN + PT), B3 co-location FAQ, B3 news archive, Portuguese-language news (migração/mudança data center, alta frequência co-location), Equinix SP migration angle, DR site (XV de Novembro), Seaborn/Anova route PRs, TNS announcement, datacentermap/baxtel listings, Nominatim geocode of the documented address. FINDINGS: Current primary matching site is unambiguous — B3 owns and operates its own data center at R. Ricardo Prudente de Aquino 85, Santana de Parnaíba (São Paulo metro), with PUMA matching engines (9 equities, 6 derivatives) physically in the same facility as the co-location racks, 10 MW site power, Tier III + LEED, ~22 µs trading-rack RTT; PUMA production migrated there Nov 2017 (B3 news, Mar 2017). NO EVIDENCE FOUND of any migration of B3 matching to a commercial colo (Equinix SP or otherwise) in EN or PT searches through Jul 2026 — Equinix's SP3/SP4 campus is coincidentally also in Santana de Parnaíba but is a separate facility; B3's own Feb-2026 press coverage (Estado de Minas) still describes B3-operated mirrored data centers and on-premise co-location. NOT FINDABLE IN PUBLIC RECORD: (1) any named quant/HFT firm's rack presence in B3 co-location — only infrastructure intermediaries are public (TNS 2023; i9DC markets B3-colocation hosting but its site was not used as evidence); (2) street address or power figure for the XV de Novembro DR data center (B3 names it and gives founding year 2008 only); (3) B3 client-portal notice 'Novidades nos serviços de Data Center e Colocation' is login-gated and unreadable; (4) datacentermap.com page rate-limited (HTTP 429), so its coordinates were not used — coordinates come from a street-level Nominatim geocode of the B3-documented address. A B3 FAQ line notes a network setup update scheduled H2-2025 (no relocation implied). Historical downtown primary site returned as a second record with the inferred former-primary role explicitly flagged.

results.reasoning: Fetched all 8 researcher evidence URLs plus 4 corroboration attempts. Both BMV PDFs were bot-opaque to the fetch model but downloaded; I extracted text locally and verified the KIO/ATG co-location sentence verbatim in the Informe Anual 2012 and the co-location/Equinix NY5 fees in the 2024 price list. Beeks, Mondo Visione, and all three KIO pages verified their quotes verbatim. Changes forced: (1) MoNeT latency corrected from ~100 to the source-stated 90 microseconds; (2) GuavaTech stripped everywhere — its Mondo Visione source is dead (ECONNRESET), the desktop mirror renders no article body, and the Equinix forum corroboration is 403; (3) arc 2 (CME Aurora - BMV, historical) dropped — the PRNewswire launch release names no medium or facilities, and the 25 Aug 2017 decommission exists only in bot-walled cmegroup.com notices with no accessible second source, so neither the arc's medium/endpoints nor its historical status can be verified; (4) BIVA's exact 25 Jul 2018 start date and the Cencor-group linkage removed as unsupported by fetched sources; (5) record 1's site name now flags the 2012 vintage of the KIO link, since no public source confirms the 2025 Beeks environment remains within KIO. Both records survive at confirmed (BMV primary documents; KIO operator pages, power_mw 1.137 source-stated), arc 1 survives with operator corrected to Grupo BMV and an explicit medium caveat. Verdict: promote_with_changes.

THE colocation-scandal case study: SEBI order of 30 Apr 2019 (WTM/GM/EFD/03/2018-19) found unfair tick-by-tick (TBT) data access via first-connect to POP/secondary servers in this facility (colo launched via NSE circular of 31 Aug 2009) and directed NSE to disgorge Rs 624.89 crore; per DCD, SEBI disposed of the charges in Sept 2024, stating no evidence suggested a violation of regulations.

results.reasoning: All five evidence URLs verified. The two nsearchives.nseindia.com PDFs time out via WebFetch (NSE bot-wall) but are live and were retrieved via curl with a browser UA, exactly as the researcher described: the 8 Jan 2025 press release (image-based PDF, read visually) verbatim confirms 'the Exchange Plaza, BKC site', 1,200+ FRE, 200+/100+ member counts, the 1,500-rack and ~2,000-FRE expansion, office-to-datacentre conversion, and the no-BCP/DR disclaimer; the corporate-reply PDF contains the exact street address. The SEBI 2019 order PDF was fetched and text-extracted: order number WTM/GM/EFD/03/2018-19, the researcher's quote verbatim (from the 31 Aug 2009 circular), Rs 624.89 crore disgorgement (note: WebFetch's summarizer misread this as 694 crore; direct text extraction proves the researcher's 624.89 is correct), OPG Securities first-connect findings, and both 'POP' and 'Secondary Server' terminology. The SEBI index page and the DCD article (published 27 Sept 2024) are live and support their claims, including the Sept 2024 disposal of charges. One unsupported specific was stripped: 'with OPG Securities' disgorgement retained' — no fetched URL mentions it (DCD does not name OPG; the researcher's source for it was unfetched press). Geography checks out: all sources place the facility at Exchange Plaza, BKC, Mumbai, India; lat/lon 19.0607/72.8612 plausibly sits in BKC; coord_precision=approximate is correctly conservative since coordinates are not source-stated. power_mw correctly null (no published MW figure found). Confidence stays 'confirmed' — strongest verified sources are primary (exchange press release + regulator order). No arcs claimed and none found; empty arcs list is correct. Verdict: promote_with_changes (one clause stripped, minor wording tightened; everything else survives as submitted).
results.research_notes: Searched: nseindia.com colocation service pages (direct WebFetch to www.nseindia.com and nsearchives timed out — NSE blocks non-browser clients; retrieved the 8 Jan 2025 press release 'Expansion of Co-location Capacity at NSEIL Premises' PDF via curl with browser UA and read it in full); SEBI enforcement order index for 'Order in the matter of NSE Colocation' (April 2019) and downloaded the actual 104-page WTM order PDF (WTM/GM/EFD/03/2018-19), text-extracted and quoted verbatim — it confirms the 2009 colo launch inside NSE premises, the TBT/secondary-POP unfair-access findings, OPG Securities (44 mentions), and the Rs 624.89 crore disgorgement; DCD article on tripling rack capacity to 4,000 at Exchange Plaza; Business Standard / Angel One coverage seen in search results but not fetched (paywall/aggregator, not needed given primary sources). NOT findable in the public record: any MW power figure for the NSE colo facility (rack counts only — power_mw left null); a full source-stated coordinate (lat/lon placed from the documented street address, coord_precision=approximate, must be geocode-verified before publication); any credibly documented, mappable microwave route between Mumbai exchange sites (searched BSE–NSE microwave links; only generic vendor/connectivity marketing such as BSO 'Mumbai Connect' — no masts, licenses, or route endpoints documented), so no arcs returned. Also of note from NSE's own disclaimer: the colo facility 'does not have a separate BCP / DR site'. The Sept 2024 SEBI disposal order (charges against NSE/executives dropped, OPG disgorgement raised to ~Rs 85 crore) is covered in press cited in search results; the 2019 order PDF remains the anchoring regulatory document. Suggested site_id: T1-011.

results.reasoning: Adversarial verification of candidate bse-mumbai. I fetched all 8 researcher evidence URLs; all are live and all support their claims. The three bseindia.com PDFs were downloaded and read page-by-page (not just summarized): ColocationOfServers.pdf contains the exact 'first Co-location facility at BSE premises with Netmagic' sentence; the Aug 2022 investor deck contains the exact NTT-Netmagic quote (p.41) plus the latency (6us exchange response, <10us colo network RTT, ~16us total), PTP-from-Grandmaster, 10G fibre, and the Greeksoft/uTrade/Resolute SMA vendor list (p.42); the Netmagic brochure contains the exact Thomson Reuters Elektron India-hub sentence. Directory listings (datacenterHawk with the full P.J. Towers street address; Cloudscene naming NTT DATA GDC 'DC3 - Mumbai (BSE)' at 1.00MW), VARIndia (2010 opening), Angel One (2025 expansion), and Estee Advisors (self-declared BSE colo broker) all verified. Confidence stays 'confirmed' — the core existence/operator/location claims rest on primary exchange documents (evidence_type exchange_docs), and coord_precision 'exact' is justified by the published full street address of an unambiguous landmark building whose coordinates (18.9296, 72.8331) match; every quote is verbatim and under 25 words. Changes made: (1) stripped the DataCenterMap 0.3 MW figure from the capacity note — that URL was never fetched (429) and the figure is unverifiable, so the note now cites only the verified Cloudscene 1.00 MW directory figure while keeping power_mw null (no operator-stated figure exists); (2) added a new corroborating source I found (NiftyTrader, May 30 2025) that quotes the BSE MD/CEO's May 6 post-earnings call for the 300-to-500 rack expansion and 15/6 kVA mix, since Angel One alone is a broker news page rather than top-tier press; (3) minor attribution tightening in capacity_note and coord_basis. BSE's own colocation.aspx page is JS-rendered and yielded no extractable text, so it was not added. No arcs claimed or warranted — the researcher correctly found no documented BSE-NSE microwave route. Verdict: promote_with_changes.
results.research_notes: Searched: BSE colocation service docs on bseindia.com (found and text-extracted four primary PDFs: Colocationbrochure.pdf, ColocationOfServers.pdf, BSENetmagicCoLocationFacility.pdf, BSE_update_August_2022.pdf — the last has full colo tech slides: NTT-Netmagic operated, <10us colo network RTT, ~6us exchange response, PTP from Grandmaster clock, 10G fibre, equal-length cables, free EOBI feed, free/cheap racks vs 'other exchange'); NTT GDC operator pages (services.global.ntt returned Header Overflow, datacenters.com 403, datacentermap.com 429 — could not fetch operator's own page, so address is anchored on datacenterHawk and Cloudscene directory listings plus BSE's own 'at BSE premises' statements); press (Business Standard 2011 article 403; Bloomberg video 2025-12-03 'BSE Sees Higher Demand for Colocation Racks' 403/paywalled — found but NOT used as evidence; AngelOne news fetched: 300 racks now, +200 planned, ~500 total, Rotunda Hall reclaimed, rack pricing). NOT findable in the public record: (1) any individual global HFT firm documented in BSE colo (India named-firm records overwhelmingly concern NSE colo; only Estee Advisors self-declares BSE colo services, plus BSE-named algo vendors Greeksoft/uTrade/Resolute); (2) a primary-source MW power figure — directories conflict (0.3 MW DataCenterMap vs 1.0 MW Cloudscene vs an implausible 25 MW on datacenters.com), so power_mw left null; (3) no documented BSE–NSE Mumbai microwave route for a Tier-2 arc — the NSE 'dark fibre' case (Sampark Infotainment) concerns preferential fiber access at NSE colo, not a mappable inter-exchange arc, so no arcs returned. Suggest a follow-up candidate: NSE colocation (Exchange Plaza BKC / Kohinoor Kurla data centre, ~1,300 racks expanding to ~4,000 per Business Standard and DCD) to pair with this pin for India.

results.reasoning: All 7 evidence URLs were fetched and verified live on 2026-07-05, and every quote appears verbatim in the source (the Colt datasheet required a direct PDF read because WebFetch's text extraction saw only image data; pages 2-3 contain all quoted sentences). Both records rest on primary operator sources — Koscom (KRX's own IT subsidiary) official pages and a Colt DCS datasheet — so 'confirmed' is the correct grade for both; The TRADE and Baxtel are corroborating only. Geography verified: the documented address (Jaseong-ro 133beon-gil 15, Dong-gu) geocodes via OSM Nominatim to 35.1380, 129.0647, matching both pins; coord_precision 'approximate' is correct (no source-stated coordinates; record 1 has no published address, only Koscom's '100 m diagonal' statement). A Korean-language search found no public record of the derivatives computing centre relocating, and Koscom's live pages still describe the Busan setup, supporting status=active. CHANGES: (1) Dropped the Busan→T1-010 arc — the sole evidence URL (Colt datasheet) verifies route (Tokyo–Busan ultra-low-latency access) and operator (Colt DCS) but never states the medium ('fiber' is an inference), does not support the 'Koscom Stock-Net' operator claim, and the researcher concedes the JPX endpoint is symbolic since Colt's Tokyo POPs are its own data centres; that fails the route+operator+medium test. (2) Stripped '(KOSPI 200 futures/options etc.)' from record 1's capacity_note — no verified source names the products. power_mw is null in both records, correctly, as no MW figure is published. No named-firm tenant claims are made, consistent with the non-public-information rule.
results.research_notes: SEARCHED: English and Korean queries — "KRX colocation Busan derivatives matching engine", "Korea Exchange Busan IT center colocation HFT", "코스콤 부산 데이터센터 주소 파생상품 코로케이션", "한국거래소 파생상품 전산센터 부산 위치", "한국거래소 부산 파생전산센터 범일동", "KRX 차세대 시스템 부산 데이터센터 이전", KVH/Koscom 2012 press. FETCHED: Koscom English trading-system page, Koscom English Derivatives Proximity Service Center page, Koscom Korean Busan Proximity DC business + directions pages (address verbatim), Colt Busan datasheet PDF (full text read), The TRADE article, Ajunews 2013 field report (states 90m from the derivatives computing centre; opened June 2012), Baxtel, Busan city news on KRX BIFC HQ move, OSM Nominatim geocode. KEY FINDING: the KRX derivatives matching engine runs from a KRX/Koscom computing centre in Beomil-dong, Dong-gu, Busan — NOT at the KRX headquarters (BIFC Landmark Building, Munhyeon-dong, Nam-gu, where the derivatives market division offices sit; that is offices, not machines, so it was not returned as a pin). Third-party access is via Koscom's Busan Proximity DC (KB Insurance Building, 15 Jaseong-ro 133beon-gil, 2F), opened 2012-06-04, ~90-100 m from the matching engine, marketed internationally by KVH (acquired by Colt) with fiber connectivity to Tokyo/Chicago/Singapore/HK/Seoul/Sydney. NOT FINDABLE IN PUBLIC RECORD: (1) a published street address or building name for the KRX derivatives computing centre itself — only Koscom's "within 100 m diagonal" statement, so its pin is approximate; (2) any power/MW figures for either facility; (3) any individually named trading firms as Busan colocation tenants (press describes customers generically as international HFT/institutional traders); (4) any public record as of 2026-07 that the matching engine has relocated — Koscom's live pages still describe the Busan setup. ACCESS ISSUES: Businesswire (KVH-Koscom launch release, 2012-02-27) returned 403/timeout and Finextra 403 — The TRADE article covers the same announcement and was used instead; datacenters.com rate-limited (429).

Does NOT name Keppel.

DCD, 27 July 2015. Verified: 'Keppel T&T's Digihub data center in Singapore was in the news recently when it was involved in an outage that affected the operations of the Singapore Stock Exchange (SGX) for more than four hours late last year.' This is the load-bearing press link placing SGX's PDC in Keppel Digihub.
Address '25 Serangoon North Ave 5' verified for Keppel DC Singapore 1 (S25). Page does not mention SGX or Digihub — supports address only.

Operator page verified: 'situated within the Serangoon North Industrial Estate, some 10.5km north of the city centre', 225,956 sq ft GFA, ABS OSPAR certification. Page does NOT mention former name Digihub or 'six-storey' — those specifics stripped or re-sourced.
Verified: 'SGX Co-Location Data Center', owner Singapore Exchange Limited, Tier III Design certification, Singapore.

The TRADE, 18 April 2011. Verified 50-clients quote verbatim; data centre 'officially opened on 11 April'; co-location service 'initial launch in June 2010'; S$250m Reach programme, 90 microsecond engine.
TNS press release, 25 May 2021. Verified SGX quote verbatim; 'round trip network latency of less than 100 microseconds'; Tier 1 data center area managed hosting. Supports service active as of 2021.
Verified: Reach live 16 Aug 2011, 'ultra-low latency of less than 90 microseconds', $250m programme, 'In April 2011... the state-of-the-art SGX Data Centre and Co-Location services' rolled out.

Arc evidence. Page does not use the word 'fiber' — medium is implied (subsea/terrestrial cable), noted in arc note. Original 26-word quote in arc note trimmed to <25 words.

Trade press corroboration (1 Mar 2018): 'BSO Upgrades Singapore-Hong Kong Network'; route provides 'the lowest latency currently available'. No medium stated.
HTTP 403 bot-wall. Not needed — BSO operator pages and WatersTechnology are accessible and cover the same facts. Not cited.

Fetched and text-extracted: it is a 2013 China Telecom Global presentation, not BSO. Shows HK-Singapore served by subsea/terrestrial cable (EAC, APCN2) generally, but wrong operator — not cited as evidence.
BSO lists 'SGX Singapore' among its colocation/exchange sites. Weak supplementary support for BSO connectivity into SGX; not cited as primary evidence.

Verified: building 'Keppel Digihub' at '25 Serangoon North Avenue 5', postal (S)554914 (formerly Uraco Building). Added as evidence backing the full address incl. postal code and the Digihub name at that address.

Verified: 'The Beeks Private Cloud is rolled out to key financial data centres and exchange locations worldwide, including NY4, LD4, TY3, SG1, CME, SGX and LME.' Backs the Beeks vendor-presence claim in firms_linked; added as evidence.

results.reasoning: Adversarial re-verification of candidate sgx-tier1-dc. All 8 record evidence URLs plus the arc URL were fetched and every one is live and supports its claim; the two PDFs (SGX BCOI release, HKEX-hosted deck) were downloaded and text-extracted rather than trusted from snippets. The SGX BCOI release verbatim confirms the Tier III footnote, the third-party-provided PDC, and the 5 Nov 2014 outage — but does not name Keppel, so the researcher's decision to grade the pin 'reported' (building placement rests on DCD press) is correct and retained. The Digihub = 25 Serangoon North Ave 5 = Keppel DC Singapore 1 chain was independently corroborated (Streetdirectory page fetched and added as evidence; Nominatim geocode re-run, coords 1.3756/103.875 confirmed, so coord_precision=exact stands on a documented full street address). Changes made: (1) stripped 'under Keppel DC REIT' from operator_or_venue and 'six-storey' from capacity_note — not on any fetched page; (2) stripped Thomson Reuters/Refinitiv from firms_linked (no evidence URL); Beeks kept after fetching and verifying its SGX page, added as evidence; (3) added Streetdirectory URL backing the full address incl. postal code; (4) capacity_note now reflects the verified June 2010 initial colo launch alongside the 11 Apr 2011 data centre opening; (5) arc kept — BSO's own pages verify route and that it is BSO's own link (operator docs = confirmed), WatersTechnology corroborates; the 26-word quote in the arc note was trimmed under 25 words, and the note now states plainly that no source uses the word 'fiber' (medium rests on BSO's 'terrestrial and subsea access points' — a cable route; no alternative medium is physically plausible HK-Singapore). Checked for staleness: no SGX relocation or new-primary-DC announcement found through July 2026; TNS 2021, Beeks, and current SGX colocation pages support status=active. power_mw stays null (the 20MW figure remains unsourced). Nothing failed verification outright, so verdict is promote_with_changes rather than reject.
results.research_notes: Searches: SGX co-location Tier 1 data centre location; SGX + Keppel Digihub/Serangoon; SGX Reach 2011 launch; SGX 5 Nov 2014 outage BCOI; named prop firms (Virtu/Optiver/Grasshopper/Flow Traders) at SGX colo; SGX DC relocation 2024-2026; BSO/Avelacom SGX routes. Fetched and verified: SGX BCOI news release PDF (links.sgx.com, primary — confirms Tier III third-party-provided Primary Data Centre but does NOT name Keppel), DCD 2015 (names Keppel T&T's Digihub as the DC whose outage halted SGX), Baxtel + Keppel operator page (KDC SGP1 = 25 Serangoon North Ave 5, Serangoon North Industrial Estate), Uptime Institute cert page, The TRADE (2011 colo launch, 50 clients incl. prop firms), TNS 2021 release, Mondovisione Reach go-live, BSO HKEX-SGX route page, Nominatim geocode (building tagged Keppel Digihub / Keppel DC Singapore 1 at 1.37560, 103.87496). NOT findable in the public record: (a) any named quant/HFT firm colocated at SGX — only the aggregate 2011 client description; (b) a primary source explicitly naming Keppel Digihub as SGX's PDC (hence confidence=reported for the pin); (c) any MW power figure from a fetched source (a 20MW figure for KDC SGP1 appeared only in an unfetched datacenters.com search snippet — excluded); (d) any relocation or new-primary-DC announcement through mid-2026 — TNS (2021) and Beeks CaaS (2020) indicate the service remains active; (e) location of SGX's Secondary Data Centre (mentioned in BCOI release, site not public — not mapped). sgx.com pages returned 403 and DataCenterMap 429, so neither is cited as evidence. Avelacom's 2024 Seoul/HK/Singapore release does not mention SGX colocation — no Avelacom arc added.

results.reasoning: Adversarial re-verification of asx-alc-gordon. All seven researcher evidence URLs were fetched and all are live and support their claims; the Cboe CXA Connectivity Manual PDF was downloaded and text-extracted locally to verify quotes verbatim (v1.0.17, (c)2026: CXA platform in Equinix SY5; Cboe Connect in ALC Meet Me Rooms; redundant shared dark fibre between data centres; SY2 nanosecond equalisation; Global Switch Ultimo DR). The researcher's key correction stands: no source places the ALC in 'Gordon NSW' — ASX pages, Nexthop, DCD and iTnews all place it at Gore Hill Business Park, Artarmon, at 5a Broadcast Way, Artarmon NSW 2064. Both coordinate pairs were independently reproduced via Nominatim (Broadcast Way street-level for ALC = approximate; building-level match at 200 Bourke Rd Alexandria for SY5 = exact per full-street-address rule). Changes made: (1) stripped the 'North Gate' address prefix — not present on any fetched page; (2) stripped 'DRUPS' and the named matching engines (ASX Trade Match, Centre Point, ASX 24) from the ALC capacity note — the fetched ASX pages say only 'primary markets and post-trade platforms'; (3) softened 'Cboe maintains a PoP inside ASX's ALC' to the manual's actual language (Cboe Connect access in ALC meet-me rooms — the manual reserves 'PoP' for SY2); (4) removed unfetched corroboration (DataCenterMap/datacenters.com listings, 'Securus' ownership already excluded by researcher) from rationales. power_mw stays null for the ALC (no public figure) and 54 for SY5 (source-stated on fetched Baxtel page, explicitly caveated as facility-wide; Equinix's own spec PDF is 403-blocked so no primary corroboration was possible). Both records remain 'confirmed' on the strength of verified primary operator/exchange documents; the single fiber arc is fully supported by the Cboe manual for route, operator and medium. Verdict: promote_with_changes.
results.research_notes: Searched: 'ASX Australian Liquidity Centre Gordon NSW', address queries (Ryde Rd/Pacific Hwy), ALC launch 2012 press, ASX microwave service, ALC-Cboe routes, Equinix SY5 address. Fetched: asx.com.au/alc and /connectivity-services/alc, Nexthop ALC page, DCD IaaS article, iTnews go-live article, Cboe CXA Connectivity Manual PDF (read locally), Baxtel SY5, Nominatim geocodes. KEY CORRECTION: the candidate's 'Gordon, NSW' is not supported anywhere in the public record — every source places the ALC at Gore Hill Business Park, Artarmon ('Gore Hill' likely mis-remembered as 'Gordon'). Verbatim address: North Gate, 5a Broadcast Way, Artarmon NSW 2064. NOT FOUND in public record: any MW power figure for the ALC (only DRUPS/Tier III design claims); endpoints of ASX's microwave connectivity service (ASX says only 'between key exchange locations' — no microwave arc claimed; an ACMA Register of Radiocommunications Licences search around Gore Hill/Alexandria would be the primary-source next step); current quant/HFT tenant names beyond the 2012 launch list (Optiver/IMC etc. not publicly documented as ALC tenants — not claimed). Dead ends: ASX-hosted AFR article PDF 404s; asxonline ALC connectivity guide PDF timed out; equinix.com SY5 page 403; DataCenterMap 429 (Baxtel used instead). Building reportedly owned by Securus (search-result mention, iTnews-derived; not independently fetched, so kept out of the record). Suggest updating existing T2/T1 tables: ALC is also the logical head-end for any future Australia arcs.

results.reasoning: Adversarial verification of candidate twse-taipei. Fetched all six researcher evidence URLs; all are live and support their attributed claims, including the two Chinese-language PDFs/articles (the supervision PDF was downloaded and text-extracted locally, confirming the exact quoted audit line 109年11月... and the colo-inside-資訊中心 diagram). Every specific in capacity_note survived sourcing: 25 Jan 2016 opening (ETtoday), NT$3.55bn (MyGoNews only — ETtoday does not carry it, so its supports scope was corrected), 7F+B3/28,647 sqm/dual Taipower/TIA-942/magnitude-7 (DCD), 4kW-5kW racks and 100Mbps lines (TWSE Q&A), equal-distance connections (TWSE colo page). power_mw correctly null (no MW figure anywhere). Geography verified: Nominatim geocodes of Guoqing Rd and Gancheng Rd bracket the given coordinate inside Banqiao District; coord_precision=approximate is correct (intersection documented, no street number). The one weakness in the researcher's package was that the Banqiao LOCATION rested on press only while primary docs confirmed only the service; I closed it by fetching TWSE's 2011 annual report (twse.com.tw PDF), which states 'the building of a Banqiao Computer Center (the first information center) has been planned' — added as evidence, so confidence=confirmed now stands on a fully primary chain (service → colo inside information center → information center in Banqiao). The TWSE press release id=18111 remains JS-rendered/unreadable and is excluded, as the researcher noted. Changes vs researcher output: added the annual-report evidence URL; removed the unsourced editorial clause 'effectively Taiwan's one execution-tier site'; corrected the ETtoday supports scope; kept firms_linked as an absence-is-the-finding statement (no firm named — compliant with the non-public-information rule). No arcs claimed and none found; researcher's no-microwave-routes finding for Taiwan stands. Verdict: promote_with_changes.
results.research_notes: Searched: TWSE co-location EN + ZH (主機共置) official pages; TWSE colo Q&A; TWSE colo supervision PDF on dsp.twse.com.tw (fetched and text-extracted locally — confirms colo room inside 證交所資訊中心, TPEx hosts co-housed, special audits of colo-using brokers from Nov 2020/109年11月); TPEx colo page (dsp.tpex.org.tw — service exists, user portal dated Dec 2017, no location stated); DCD article on the Banqiao data center opening; ETtoday opening-day report (25 Jan 2016); MyGoNews real-estate report giving the Guoqing Rd x Gancheng Rd intersection, 1,850-ping site, NT$3.55bn cost; OSM Nominatim geocoding of both roads (intersection ~24.994, 121.459, Qingshui area near Far Eastern Hospital MRT — consistent with CHT's adjacent Banqiao Cloud Data Center description). NOT findable in the public record: (1) a street number for the TWSE Banqiao data center — only the intersection is published, so coord_precision stays approximate; (2) the exact go-live date of the colo service (facility opened Jan 2016 with colo announced as planned; TPEx colo portal dates from Dec 2017; TWSE audits confirm active use by Nov 2020); (3) any MW power figure (only 4kW/5kW per rack); (4) any individually named quant/HFT firm using TWSE colo — eligibility is member securities firms and information vendors only, so foreign HFT access is intermediated by brokers, and no firm-level public link exists (absence is the finding); (5) TWSE press release id=18111 is JS-rendered and would not yield text; rootlaw mirror of the colo management rules returned 403 — the TWSE regulation database confirms the rules (FL086480, version effective 2021-05-11) but the original enactment date was not extractable. Note for the map: TWSE HQ is at Taipei 101 (7 Xinyi Rd Sec 5) but the matching engine and colo room are in Banqiao, New Taipei City — pin Banqiao, not the HQ. No Tier-2 arcs: no public record of microwave/mm-wave relay routes serving Taiwanese venues was found.

Official JSE brochure PDF (text extracted locally after fetch): Tier 3 design, 35 racks in expansion Phases 1-4 plus 38 more = 73 racks, ~20us vs 2400us Sandton, 3kW/5kW feeds, 16 NSPs in meet-me-room, equal-length cabling, London PoP connectivity fees. Does NOT contain a 2014 launch date — researcher wrongly listed launch date among facts this URL supports.

DCD (21 May 2014): JSE 'has launched its colo center' on JSE premises; 'roundtrip latency of 150μs compared to the existing 2550μs'; Millennium IT software. Does NOT mention 35 hosting units, a 12 May date, Tier 3, or Sandton — those specifics stripped or re-sourced.
JSE Magazine: 20% of trading at 2014 launch, ~65% by mid-2022, 71 racks, ~43-rack expansion planned for early 2023, Tier 3 design. Note 71 racks here vs 73 in the 2021 brochure.
JSE announcement (19 Jun 2023): Colo 2.0 collaboration with Beeks Group and IPC Systems; confirms 2014 launch of the colocation centre in the JSE's data centre; quoted sentence verified verbatim.

results.reasoning: All five evidence URLs fetched and verified live and supporting. The brochure PDF required local text extraction (pypdf) after the fetch model failed to parse the binary; extracted text confirms Tier 3, 73 racks, ~20us vs 2400us, 3kW/5kW, 16 NSPs, meet-me-room, equal-length cabling, London PoP fees. Record survives at confidence=confirmed (multiple primary exchange docs plus corroborating press). Changes made: (1) stripped 'Launched 12 May 2014 with 35 hosting units' — no fetched source states the 12 May date or 35 units at launch; DCD article (dated 21 May 2014) contains neither figure, and the brochure's 35 racks belong to expansion Phases 1-4, not launch; replaced with 'Launched May 2014'. (2) Removed 'uniform pricing' (attributed to Traders Magazine, not in the evidence set). (3) Removed 'data vendors' and 'much international demand UK-based' client characterizations — not present in any fetched page; replaced with brochure-verified sub-letting rules (Members, their clients, accredited SIPs). (4) Corrected the brochure's 'supports' field, which wrongly claimed it backs the 2014 launch date; that fact is now sourced to DCD and the JSE 2023 announcement. (5) Trimmed the DCD quote to the exactly-verified text (the '...for clients in Sandton' tail could not be confirmed and DCD does not mention Sandton). (6) Noted the 71-rack (JSE Magazine 2022) vs 73-rack (brochure 2021) discrepancy in the capacity note. Geography: Sandown/Sandton, Johannesburg matches the JSE's own published address; lat/lon (-26.105, 28.058) plausibly sits at Sandton CBD; coord_precision stays 'approximate' since coordinates are address-placed, not source-stated. power_mw correctly null (only per-rack 3kW/5kW published). Empty arcs correct: the London PoP is a connectivity product with no published physical endpoint, so no route can be drawn. Quotes all under 25 words and verified verbatim.
results.research_notes: Searched: 'JSE colocation centre launch 2014', 'JSE colocation One Exchange Square Sandton address', 'JSE colocation clients HFT firms named'. Fetched and verified: JSE colocation service page (jse.co.za/services/jse-colocation-connectivity), official JSE colocation brochure PDF (2021 — Tier 3, 73 racks, 3kW/5kW units, ~20µs vs 2,400µs, 16 NSPs, meet-me-room, London PoP), JSE Colo 2.0 announcement (Beeks/IPC, June 2023), DCD launch report (May 2014, 150µs vs 2,550µs, 35 hosting units per search snippet), JSE Magazine 'Well Connected' (20% of trading at launch → ~65% by mid-2022; 71 racks; ~43-rack expansion planned for early 2023), Traders Magazine 'Democratic Colocation' (equal 40m cable lengths, uniform pricing), A-Team/Absa HFT pieces. NOT findable in the public record: (1) any individual trading firm named as a JSE colo client — JSE only characterizes clients generically ('Top 10 firms', members, MSPs; ITWeb Africa search snippet mentions two unnamed India-based prop firms at ~5% of JSE volume, but the fetched Absa version of the article does not contain that claim, so it was not used); (2) any site-level MW power figure (only per-rack 3kW/5kW feeds); (3) source-stated coordinates — lat/lon are placed from the documented street address and must be geocode-verified before publication. Engineering News and Finextra launch articles returned HTTP 403 and were not used as evidence. No documented Tier-2 arc endpoints: the brochure references a JSE London PoP (connectivity product) but does not state the PoP's physical location, so no arc was returned.

results.reasoning: Fetched all four researcher evidence URLs; all are live and support their claims. (1) PR Newswire TASE Q2-2020 results: verbatim quote confirmed — colocation/hosting for trading servers 'at TASE's Data Center' — corporate primary source. (2) Electra contractor page: confirms Tier III-equivalent computer room, 1,000 sqm, 1,200-ton air-cooled chillers, address Ahuzat Bait 2 Tel Aviv-Yafo, built 2012-2014 — contractor primary source. (3) The TRADE: researcher's quote confirmed verbatim; also confirms Avelacom PoP and NY-Tel Aviv <117ms RTT; does not say 'fiber', so that word was stripped from the capacity note. (4) FF News: TASE exec quote confirmed; kept as redundant corroboration of firms_linked. Changes made: trimmed zip 6525216 from the address (not on the cited contractor page; only street+city are verbatim); removed unverified 'fiber' characterization; added a fifth verified source found during checking (Xnet/Ynet architecture feature with on-site reporting placing the computing center on floor -4 of the building), which strengthens the building-houses-data-center link; tightened the capacity note wording. Confidence stays 'confirmed' because the strongest verified sources are primary (corporate announcement + contractor docs) for each component claim; the rationale explicitly flags the one triangulated join (colo suite = the HQ computer room), consistent with project rule 4 that absence/inference must be disclosed. Geography checks out: lat/lon 32.0633/34.7692 sits ~50m from the TASE building in central Tel Aviv — kept as approximate with geocode-before-publication flag. Arcs: researcher returned none, correctly — Avelacom's NY-Tel Aviv route has no publicly specified endpoint matching an existing site_id and medium/route detail is not source-verified, so the empty arc set stands. No MW figure exists in the public record; power_mw=null is correct.
results.research_notes: Searched: 'TASE colocation data center', 'TASE co-location Ahuzat Bayit', site:tase.co.il colocation, TASE annual-report colocation, Hebrew queries (קולוקיישן חוות שרתים בורסה אחוזת בית), Avelacom-TASE press coverage, and outsourcing checks (MedOne/Bynet). Confirmed: TASE launched colocation + hosting of trading servers at 'TASE's Data Center' in 2019-2020 (TASE Q2-2020 results release via PR Newswire; colo revenue also cited in later results); Avelacom PoP with colocation/HaaS in 'TASE's primary data center' (The TRADE + FF News, Mar 2023, incl. TASE exec quote); building computer room documented by contractor Electra: Tier III-equivalent, ~1,000 sqm, 1,200-ton chillers, Ahuzat Bait 2, Tel Aviv (built 2012-2014, USD 12m project). NOT findable in public record: MW power figure; named quant/HFT tenant firms (only 'international institutions'); exact floor/hall of colo suite; location of TASE's off-site hot-backup DR site (described only as 'at a distance from Tel Aviv' — absence is the finding); TASE's own connectivity/colocation web pages are JS-rendered and returned no text to WebFetch; WFE Focus interview with CEO Ittai Ben-Zeev (which per search snippets says colocation launched 'a few months ago' for 'local and international members... with emphasis on foreign traders') returned HTTP 403, so it is cited only in these notes, not as record evidence. Coordinates are memory-placed from the documented address and must be geocode-verified before publication. No documented Tier-2 arcs: Avelacom's NY-Tel Aviv <117ms route is fiber with no publicly specified NJ-side endpoint matching an existing site_id, so no arc returned.

results.reasoning: Adversarial verification of all 9 evidence URLs: every one is live and supports its claim. The LSEG connectivity guide PDF failed inline parsing but was downloaded and text-extracted locally — it is genuine (v2.6, 26 June 2026, 37 pages) and confirms the load-bearing claims: Spot & Forwards Matching primary London LD4 / DR Equinix NY6; NDF Matching primary Singapore SG3 / DR London LD4; venue datacentres LD4/TY3/NY4+NY5/SG1; the NY4/NY5 colo recommendation; and street addresses for LD4, NY4/NY5/NY6, TY3 and SG3 (independently matching PeeringDB). CME's wiki confirms EBS engines primary in Secaucus NY5 and Slough LD4.2 with mutual DR, and its Tokyo TY3 row lists no trading engine — corroborating the 'Tokyo engine retired' narrative that trade press (Traders Magazine, FlexTrade) supplies historically. One substantive researcher error corrected: the guide gives FXall's primary location as NJ2 (Centersquare EWR2), not TY3 — TY3 is only a cross-connect access point for FXall (PriceStream does have a TY3 primary); fixed in the TY3 record's operator_or_venue and capacity_note. Strengthened the TY3 record with the CME EBS-functionality page as direct evidence for the no-Tokyo-engine claim, and added the guide-stated LD4 street address. All confidence grades re-checked: each record's core location claim rests on fetched primary operator/exchange documentation, so 'confirmed' stands. power_mw correctly null everywhere (no source states MW). Coordinates are plausible for their cities (Secaucus NJ, Slough Trading Estate, Edagawa Koto-ku, Ayer Rajah Crescent) and correctly kept 'approximate' pending geocode verification per project rules. Arcs: route and operator verified by primary docs for all four; no source states a medium, but all four links are intercontinental so fiber is the only physically possible medium — kept with notes explicitly flagging the medium as inferred by necessity and the primary/DR pairings as venue relationships rather than documented cables. Verdict: promote_with_changes.
results.research_notes: SEARCHED: (1) EBS Market matching + NY4/LD4/TY3; (2) CME EBS TY3 Tokyo colocation; (3) LSEG/Refinitiv Matching engine locations; (4) CME EBS Tokyo matching engine retirement; (5) Equinix TY3 and SG3 addresses. FETCHED AND VERIFIED: CME Group Client Systems Wiki 'EBS Market on CME Globex Market Functionality' (EBS engines: NY FX Spot + Metals primary Equinix Secaucus NY5 / DR LD4.2; London FX Spot + NDFs primary LD4.2 / DR NY5; Tokyo listed with no trading engines); CME wiki 'CME Globex Hub - Tokyo' (EBS-only access for TY3/TY5 customers) and 'EBS Market and eFix DR for Connectivity Customers' (access via NY5, LD4.2, TY5, GLink-Secaucus/Slough); LSEG 2019 press release (Matching primary LD4, secondary NY4 campus); LSEG FX Venues Colo Connectivity Technical Deployment Guide v2.6, 26 June 2026 (full PDF extracted: venue datacentres = LD4, TY3, NY4/NY5, SG1; Spot & Forwards Matching primary London LD4 with DR in Equinix NY6; NDF Matching primary Singapore SG3 with DR LD4; PriceStream primary NY6/LD4/TY3; legacy Centersquare EWR2/NJ2 being phased out); LSEG 2022 Singapore NDF Matching announcement; Traders Magazine and FlexTrade for the legacy tri-site (NY4/LD4/TY3) EBS architecture; PeeringDB for TY3 (1-9-20 Edagawa, Tokyo 135-0051) and SG3 (26A Ayer Rajah Crescent, 139963) addresses/coords. IMPORTANT NUANCE FOR THE ESSAY: the classic 'tri-site by design' FX picture is now partly historical — EBS's Tokyo matching engine was retired in the CME Globex migration (matching consolidated to NY5 + LD4.2, Tokyo demoted to an access hub), and LSEG spot Matching is LD4-primary/NY6-DR rather than three co-equal engines; the third 'follow the sun' site today is LSEG's NDF Matching in Singapore SG3. COULD NOT FETCH / NOT FOUND: cmegroup.com-hosted PDFs (ebs-market-connectivity-options.pdf, ebs-connectivity-policies.pdf) and cmegroup.com/confluence timed out repeatedly (Atlassian-hosted mirrors used instead); equinix.com TY3 page and datacenters.com returned 403 (addresses verified via PeeringDB instead); the LSEG 2022 press release does not itself name the Singapore data centre (SG3 comes from the 2026 guide); no public source found stating MW capacity for any of these sites (power_mw left null); pre-2019 Reuters/Refinitiv legacy Matching engine sites (e.g. old NJ/Singapore nodes) were not researched beyond the 2019 relocation release. Wikipedia not used. All quotes verbatim from fetched pages; extraction artifacts in the LSEG PDF (stray spaces) normalized.

results.reasoning: Adversarial verification largely upheld the researcher's work. All 10 original evidence URLs were fetched and all are live and support their claims: the MOEX colocation connectivity guide PDF was downloaded and its text extracted locally, confirming verbatim 'hall 202 of the DataSpace Data Center, Sharikopodshipnikovskaya st 11 building 9, Moscow 115088' and 'valid since 10 October 2022'; MOEX press releases n6736 (2014) and n23779 (2019), the s653 network-access page (both DC addresses verbatim), the 2022 annual report (non-resident cutoff), DCD 2014 (9.5MW, four floors, Tier III Gold), Finance Magnates 2016 (DataSpace1 primary / M1 DR cutover) and 2015 (M1 HFT colo), Markets Media 2012 (TMX Atrium fiber, M1 hosting Micex-RTS), and the M1Cloud operator page (address, NICEVT, since 2006) all check out. Changes made: (1) two quotes trimmed to verified verbatim fragments (DCD quote's 'in our decision' tail and n6736's spliced 'DataSpace1' prefix were not confirmed contiguous); (2) stripped unverified embellishments — 'Russia's first' Tier III Gold, 'lying skyscraper' nickname, 'Stack Group'/'Stack.M1' branding, and the '~2011' era start (the 2012 source uses future tense); (3) the arc's fiber medium is NOT stated in the MOEX evidence URL, so I found and fetched an accessible corroborating source (Traders Magazine, 19 Dec 2016: Avelacom London-Moscow route, 900km terrestrial fibre, serving Moscow Exchange order flow) and cited it in the arc note — route and operator remain primary-confirmed by the MOEX release, and arc origin T1-005 verified as Equinix LD4 in data/sites_seed.csv. Confidence grades stand at confirmed for both records (strongest verified sources are MOEX's own documents) and the arc. power_mw=9.5 is source-stated (DCD); M1 power is null. Geography checks pass: both addresses are source-verbatim, both lat/lon plausibly sit at those Moscow addresses, coord_precision correctly left at approximate with geocoding flagged as required before publication. firms_linked correctly names no individual firm (none publicly documented), per project rule 4. Status=historical preserved per the candidate's framing (international HFT destination ended Feb 2022), with the facilities' continued physical operation noted.
results.research_notes: Searched: MOEX + DataSpace1/M1 migration (EN), MOEX colocation guides, DCD/Finance Magnates/Markets Media archives, Avelacom London–Moscow route, 2022 foreign-HFT exodus coverage. Fetched and verified: MOEX press releases n6736 (2014) and n23779 (2019), MOEX network-access page s653 (both DC addresses verbatim), MOEX Colocation Connectivity Guide PDF Oct 2022 (colo = hall 202, DataSpace1, Sharikopodshipnikovskaya 11 bld 9 — extracted locally from the downloaded PDF), DCD 2014 + 2016 articles (timeline: Kislovsky DR -> DataSpace1 DR Jul 2016 -> DataSpace1 primary Nov 2016, M1 to DR), Finance Magnates Nov 2016 cutover piece, Markets Media 2012 (TMX Atrium London–Stockholm–Moscow fiber, ~40ms, terminating at M1), MOEX 2022 annual report (non-residents unable to trade from end-Feb 2022). NOT findable in the public record: (1) any named Western HFT firm (Jump, Optiver, XTX, etc.) publicly documented as holding racks in M1 or DataSpace1 — firms_linked therefore names none, per project rule 4; (2) a dedicated press piece on the HFT-colo exodus specifically — the 2022 cutoff is evidenced via the CBR non-resident ban as reported in MOEX's own annual report and the UK's May 2022 derecognition (RFE/RL page returned 403, so not used as evidence). DataCenterMap pages for both DCs returned 429, so no source-stated coordinates: both lat/lon are placed from documented addresses, coord_precision=approximate, and must be geocode-verified before publication per project rule 3. A second candidate arc (TMX Atrium London–Moscow via Stockholm, 2012) was omitted because the source does not name the London endpoint DC. Both facilities remain physically operational (DataSpace1 is still MOEX's primary DC per the Oct 2022 guide); status=historical encodes the map's framing of Moscow as a former international HFT destination, per the task's special instructions.

results.reasoning: Fetched all 8 researcher evidence URLs plus 5 additional sources. Six URLs verified live and supporting; the two city-data.com FCC-mirror pages returned HTTP 429 on repeated attempts and the FCC ULS application page timed out, so nothing accessible verifies New Line Networks' Carteret/Secaucus path endpoints — both NLN arcs are dropped (absence is the finding; a browser-based ULS check could restore them later). An FCC Public Notice PDF (DOC-390804A1) was fetched and parsed as primary confirmation that NLN holds Microwave Industrial/Business Pool licenses (WRCR823, WQXI977, etc.), but it contains no locations, so it only supplements the NLN site record. One misattributed quote found and corrected: 'poles equipped with small dish antennas...' does not appear in the DCK land-war article; replaced with verified verbatim text (single pole with two antennas; JV license held by World Class + KCG unit). Unsupported specifics stripped: IMC/Tower Research 'minority stakes' (operator page shows only an 'agreement with IMC'), Pierce Broadband Oct 2015 lease, 'western head-end of NLN's Chicago-NJ network' framing, and the fiber-tail rationale on the tower record. Vigilant Global removed from the Webline arc operator because drw.com no longer lists Vigilant and no fetched source ties Vigilant to this route; operator is Webline Holdings LLC (DRW) per Bloomberg citing FCC records — graded reported. All three McKay arcs stand at confirmed: the operator media page (fetched twice) explicitly documents the microwave medium, the Aurora origin, and the Carteret/Secaucus-NY2/Mahwah endpoints with one-way latencies; the Secaucus arc's evidence_url was swapped from the A-Team press piece to the operator doc so the confirmed grade rests on a verified primary source. Seed IDs cross-checked against data/sites_seed.csv (T1-001 Mahwah, T1-002 Carteret, T1-003 Secaucus, T1-004 CyrusOne Aurora, T2-003 McKay Aurora head-end) — arc references correct. McKay record coords left null with coord_precision=city_level pending geocoding (merges into T2-003, which retains its approximate seed coordinate); the two Aurora-area records keep approximate coords that plausibly sit in Aurora per the sources. Verdict: promote_with_changes — 3 of 3 records survive with corrections, 4 of 6 arcs survive.
results.research_notes: SEARCHES RUN: operator-specific queries (McKay Brothers Aurora/Carteret latency; New Line Networks JV; Vigilant Global DRW FCC; Webline Holdings DRW; World Class Wireless; Geodesic Networks KCG), FCC ULS call-sign queries (WQQH613, WQXI977), and Sniper in Mahwah archive queries. FETCHED AND VERIFIED: mckay-brothers.com/media (operator route/latency doc: Aurora + Cermak PoPs; Carteret, Secaucus NY2/4/5, Mahwah PoPs; QED one-way latencies 3.982-4.015 ms), a-teaminsight.com (2014 roadmap Aurora-Secaucus <8.05 ms RT, Aurora-Mahwah route in development, NJ footprint incl. Carteret), PRNewswire Carteret-NY2 release, newlinenet.com/about (JV of World Class Wireless + Geodesic Networks/KCG), Yahoo Finance KCG press release (NLN JV, Feb 12 2015), Data Center Knowledge x2 (Bloomberg land-war and tower pieces: World Class Wireless $14M/31-acre purchase across from CyrusOne Aurora I; Pierce Broadband/McKay 350-ft tower lease Oct 2015; Webline Holdings Nov 2015 license and Aurora-Carteret network; CyrusOne 350-ft tower licensees = Jefferson Microwave/McKay, NLN as Jump+Virtu JV, Webline = DRW Holdings per FCC records), digitalfinanceanalytics.com (verbatim Bloomberg text), sniperinmahwah.wordpress.com (Vigilant bought by DRW; via aliases 'built very nice paths between New Jersey and Chicago/Aurora'; NLN European licensing), drw.com/our-subsidiaries (network arm now branded DRW NX; Vigilant name no longer listed). NOT FETCHABLE (flag for manual verification before publication build): FCC ULS wireless2.fcc.gov pages (Access Denied to non-browser; NLN paths summaries WQQH613 licKey=3430566 and WQXI977 licKey=3792367 exist per search results), city-data.com FCC tower mirrors (Cloudflare challenge; search snippets show NLN call signs at Carteret WQWF994/WQWH561/WQXM804/WQXM997/WQXF496 and Secaucus WQVP918/WQWF998/WRCR823, plus Geodesic Networks at Amquip Complex Carteret WQQK214), Bloomberg 'Gazillion-Dollar Standoff' (403; content obtained via DCK republication), Crain's Chicago Business (paywalled), fwbusiness.com (HTTP 451). NOT FOUND IN PUBLIC RECORD THIS SESSION: an NLN Aurora-Mahwah endpoint; specific Vigilant/Webline Mahwah or Secaucus endpoints (only Aurora-Carteret is press-documented); no intermediate relay towers added per instructions (none individually verified here). ADJACENT FINDINGS FOR OTHER CANDIDATES: Scientel Solutions 195-ft tower next to CyrusOne Aurora (DCK/DCD document the CyrusOne-Scientel litigation and settlement) omitted as Scientel is a comms integrator, not a quant operator; Bloomberg attributes the Kane County/West Chicago shortwave project to New Line Networks — relevant to existing site T2-004 but not verified here. Anova Financial Networks corporate release, 27 Jul 2022: 'delivers its proprietary wireless platform from Mahwah, New Jersey direct to NY4' at '755 Secaucus, New Jersey'; 'core routes connecting Mahwah and Carteret'; backbone 'blankets the New Jersey Equity Triangle'. Primary source for arc 3 route/operator and arc 1 route status — but it states only 'wireless'/'10Gbps radios', NOT mm-wave, so it does not support the researcher's mm-wave medium on arc 3.

Operator's current site: 'proprietary hybrid laser radios' and 'New Jersey connectivity triangle' with 10Gbps wireless. Supports laser medium and present-day active status of the triangle. No millimeter-wave mention anywhere, contradicting the mm-wave label on arc 3.

arcs.note: 2014 launch coverage (Fibre Systems): 'the laser network that covers the 35 miles between the data centres of the NYSE in Mahwah, New Jersey' and Nasdaq Carteret; AOptix-based hybrid FSO laser + millimetre-wave. Route confirmed still active in Anova's 27 Jul 2022 corporate release: 'core routes connecting Mahwah and Carteret' (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/anova-financial-networks-activates-wireless-services-on-10gbps-backbone-between-mahwah-direct-to-ny4-301594385.html); Anova's site currently markets 'proprietary hybrid laser radios'.

A-Team Insight, 3 Aug 2015: 'hybrid laser market data link between the Secaucus and Carteret, New Jersey data centres' (Nasdaq and Bats/Direct Edge matching engines), AOptix Intellimax ULL3000 units. Press-only for this specific leg; Anova's 27 Jul 2022 release says its 10Gbps backbone 'blankets the New Jersey Equity Triangle', which implies but does not explicitly name this leg — hence reported, not confirmed.

Anova corporate release, 27 Jul 2022: 'delivers its proprietary wireless platform from Mahwah, New Jersey direct to NY4' at '755 Secaucus, New Jersey' over a 'proprietary 10Gbps wireless backbone'. Release states only 'wireless' — no specific medium named; medium set to laser per the operator's own platform description on anovafn.com: 'proprietary hybrid laser radios'. Researcher's mm-wave label had no verified source and was removed.

results.reasoning: Fetched all three evidence URLs plus anovafn.com (cited in researcher notes); all live and on-topic. Arc endpoints T1-001/T1-002/T1-003 verified present in data/sites_seed.csv (NYSE Mahwah, Nasdaq Carteret, Equinix NY4/5/6 Secaucus), so no new site records are needed and the empty records array stands — no relay/rooftop sites are publicly documented with addresses, and absence is the finding there. Arc 1 (Mahwah–Carteret): Fibre Systems press verifies route+operator+laser medium at 2014 launch, and the verified 27 Jul 2022 Anova corporate release (primary) independently confirms the route as an active core route, justifying confirmed. Arc 2 (Carteret–Secaucus): only credible specialist press (A-Team Insight 2015) explicitly names this leg; the 2022 corporate release only implies it via the 'Equity Triangle' phrase, so reported is the correct grade — kept as researcher had it, with the inference now flagged in the note. Arc 3 (Mahwah–NY4 Secaucus): route and operator are confirmed by the verified 2022 corporate release, but the researcher's mm-wave medium is unsupported — the release says only 'wireless'/'10Gbps radios' and Anova's own site describes 'proprietary hybrid laser radios' with no mm-wave mention, so the medium was corrected to laser per the operator's own documentation. Status=active is supported by the 2022 release plus Anova's current site still marketing the New Jersey connectivity triangle. Verdict is promote_with_changes: all three arcs survive, but with the arc 3 medium fixed, notes rewritten to separate verified quotes from inference, and all quotes kept under 25 words.
results.research_notes: Searched: 'Anova laser network NYSE Mahwah Nasdaq Carteret wireless link'; 'Anova Financial Networks millimeter wave Secaucus Carteret Mahwah'; WSJ 2014 'High-Speed Stock Traders Turn to Laser Beams'. Fetched and verified: fibre-systems.com FSO article (Mahwah-Carteret 35-mile laser network, endpoints verbatim); A-Team Insight 3 Aug 2015 (Secaucus-Carteret hybrid laser link, AOptix Intellimax ULL3000); PR Newswire 27 Jul 2022 Anova release (Mahwah direct to NY4 at 755 Secaucus on 10Gbps wireless backbone; 'core routes connecting Mahwah and Carteret'; backbone 'blankets the New Jersey Equity Triangle'); anovafn.com homepage ('New Jersey connectivity triangle', 'proprietary hybrid laser radios'). NOT findable in the public record: Anova's original press pages on anovanetworks.com now 301-redirect to the anovafn.com homepage with the specific route content gone; optics.org/press/2841 (AOptix-Anova NASDAQ-BATS laser release) is 404; CNBC and Morningstar mirrors returned 403; WSJ original is paywalled (its content is corroborated via Fibre Systems/Electro Optics trade coverage). No individual relay/rooftop sites (the 2014 coverage mentions lasers atop apartment buildings/towers along the 35-mile path) are publicly documented with verifiable addresses, so no new site records returned — arcs reference existing T1-001/T1-002/T1-003 endpoints. FCC ULS Part 101 licensee search for Anova shell entities was not performed in this pass (web UI not fetchable); flagging as a possible future confirmation path for the mm-wave legs.

results.reasoning: Adversarial verification of candidate channel-swingate-dunkirk. I fetched all six evidence URLs; all are live and each contains the quoted material supporting its claim, so both records and both arcs survive. I independently recomputed the OSGB36 grid reference TR 3347 4287 (from the fetched Kent HER record) to WGS84 and got 51.13747/1.33602 — an exact match to the researcher's Swingate coordinates, which plausibly sit at Swingate NE of Dover; Tour de Reuze coordinates are source-stated in the ANFR-derived record and sit in central Dunkerque, so coord_precision=exact is justified there (source-stated coords plus a documented full street address). Confidence grades stand at 'reported' for both records: the primary sources (Kent HER designation, ANFR-derived site record) confirm only location/heritage/building facts, while the HFT-usage and firm-identity claims rest on credible specialist press (Laumonier's Sniper in Mahwah fieldwork, Bloomberg via Traders Magazine citing Ofcom) — no spectrum license was fetched directly, so no upgrade to 'confirmed'. Changes made: (1) removed New Line Networks from Swingate firms_linked — no fetched source places NLN at Swingate, and Visionscarto instead places NLN on the 2016 Oostduinkerke-Richborough attempt; (2) stripped the unsourced '~141 m above sea level' figure from the Swingate capacity note; (3) narrowed arc 1 operator from 'Jump Trading / New Line Networks' to 'Jump Trading', which is what the evidence URL verifies, and deleted the 'Houtem tower bought by Jump for EUR 5m' claim from the arc note because the fetched Bloomberg reprint states the mayor did not know the buyer's identity (the EUR 5m price is attributed to Belgian officials, not tied to Jump in the verified passage); (4) softened Tour de Reuze firms_linked since no fetched source names the dish owners on that specific building — SiM 2014 leaves them unnamed and Visionscarto's McKay/Optiver linkage is to the Dunkerque area generally; (5) added explicit caveats that no post-2016 public source re-confirms the dishes in service (status 'active' reflects latest public documentation, as the researcher disclosed). Both arc endpoints resolve: dest T2-002 (Houtem mast) exists in data/sites_seed.csv, and the other endpoints are site_names of records returned here. Medium 'microwave' is verified by the 7.448-7.484 GHz path detail and dish-based descriptions. power_mw is null on both records. The researcher's omissions (Calais Mollien tower, Oostduinkerke, Ramsgate relay, paywalled Bloomberg 2019) were appropriate under project rules. Verdict: promote_with_changes.
results.research_notes: SEARCHED: Sniper in Mahwah 'HFT in my backyard' II and V (both fetched, verbatim quotes extracted); Visionscarto 'HFT in the Jungle' (fetched); Bloomberg 2019 'Gazillion-Dollar Standoff' (403 paywall — NOT fetched, not used); Traders Magazine reprint of Bloomberg July 2014 NATO-towers piece (fetched — Arqiva ownership, Optiver Ofcom license near Dover, Jump/Houtem EUR 5m); Historic England list entries 1403955/1405535 (403 — substituted the fetched Kent HER record DKE21505, grid ref TR 3347 4287, converted to WGS84 51.13747/1.33602 via OSGB36->WGS84 Helmert routine); RNC Mobile ANFR-derived record for Tour de Reuze (fetched — address + coordinates); French-language search for Tour du Reuze antennas. KEY DISAMBIGUATION: in 'HFT in my backyard V', the 'Dunkirk' hosting McKay/Latent/Optiver/Custom Connect is Dunkirk, KENT (UK Chain Home site on the RAF 1930s route inland from Swingate) — NOT Dunkerque, France; do not conflate when extending the dataset. NOT FOUND / OMITTED: (1) Calais 'Mollien' tower — Laumonier only speculates Jump 'may have used' it pre-Houtem (coords 50°57'23.31\"N 1°52'19.86\"E given in his post) — too weak to map; (2) no sourced operator pairing for a Richborough(T2-001)-to-continent arc: Visionscarto documents Vigilant/Jump/New Line 'trying to cross the Channel between Oostduinkerke and Richborough' (2016, planned; the 300 m Richborough masts were then refused by Dover District Council per SiM 'Banana Land' series) — an Oostduinkerke record would need dedicated sourcing; (3) Houtem-Ramsgate path 'allowing access to Basildon' (T1-008) is documented by Laumonier but Ramsgate relay itself is unmapped — candidate for a future record; (4) no post-2016 public source fetched confirming the Swingate/Tour-de-Reuze dishes remain in service — status 'active' reflects latest public documentation; verify against Ofcom fixed-links register and ANFR Cartoradio before publication build.

Verifies Tour du Reuze (Optiver, McKay, probably Vigilant — NO Latent Networks: stripped), grain elevator, Oostduinkerke building, Ramsgate planning applications, Houtem $5m/243m/Dec 2013. Vigilant's Richborough-Oostduinkerke crossing is described as PROPOSED, not operational.
Verifies Dunkerque(-Grande-Synthe)-Ramsgate crossing (McKay, Optiver), Cartoradio derivation, and Calais operators (McKay, Jump Trading, New Line Networks) having left Calais.

Verifies 'now they can go directly to Ramsgate, and Basildon' re Jump/Houtem. Does NOT explicitly state a Swingate bypass — that over-read stripped from the Swingate record. Sept 2014 source cannot support 'New Line Networks' as arc operator — trimmed.

Bloomberg News syndication; verifies Jump Trading bought the Houtem tower via U.K. affiliate Toren Navo Aansluiting Ltd, 800-ft (243m), 5 million euros per Belgian officials, ex-US armed forces tower.

Operator's own release (SOURCE Vigilant, 14 Aug 2017): confirms no appeal of Dover District Council's refusal; 322m mast at Richborough, Sandwich. Supports 'confirmed' grade for the status correction.

Operator release (10 Mar 2015): 'McKay's latency between Slough-LD4 and Frankfurt-FR2 is 4.64 milliseconds (ms) round trip, the lowest known'; medium explicitly microwave. Supports confirmed arc T1-005 to T1-006.
