results.reasoning: Adversarial verification of candidate london-frankfurt-hops. All 10 evidence URLs were fetched and are live; every record's core location claim survived, so verdict is promote_with_changes rather than reject. Changes made: (1) Tour du Reuze — stripped 'Latent Networks' from firms_linked; not present in either cited source. (2) Flobecq — corrected coord_basis: the 50.763892,3.744626 coordinates come from a reader comment (Kenton Ahrens) on HFT-in-my-backyard-V, not from Laumonier's article; kept as approximate since plausibly at the ex-DCS site near Flobecq. Jump linkage re-anchored to HFT-V's explicit verbatim sentence naming Flow Traders and Jump, because a pronoun-ambiguity in HFT-II made 'they co-locate' insufficient on its own. (3) Swingate — removed the second evidence item: the part-I quote ('now they can go directly to Ramsgate, and Basildon') does not explicitly state a Swingate bypass; capacity note softened accordingly. (4) Oostduinkerke — status downgraded active to historical: the Banana Land post describes Vigilant's Richborough-Oostduinkerke crossing as proposed ('impossible to build a path from Richborough without something that doesn't exist - yet') and the UK mast was refused, so no evidence the site was ever an operational crossing point. (5) Houtem-to-Flobecq arc DROPPED: no fetched source verifies a direct hop between those towers (researcher conceded this); replaced with a better-evidenced Wavre-to-Hannut arc based on HFT-II's explicit dish-orientation statement ('two pointing to Hannut'). (6) Houtem-Ramsgate arc operator trimmed to Jump Trading (Sept 2014 source predates New Line Networks). (7) Wavre and Hannut — Jump linkages strengthened to explicit named-firm quotes ('one other tower housing Jump, in Wavre'; 'Jump added a third dish in Hannut'), removing the researcher's hedge. (8) Calais — Jump Trading and New Line Networks linkage verified verbatim in Visionscarto and retained. (9) Richborough — Vigilant's own PR Newswire release verified (SOURCE Vigilant, Aug 2017): confirmed grade justified; refusal date fixed to January 2017 per part-6 eyewitness account; quote tightened to a strict substring. Local seed CSV checked: T1-005 (Equinix LD4), T1-006 (Equinix FR2), T2-001 (Richborough, currently mislabeled active/confirmed), T2-002 (Houtem) all exist, so merge ids and arc endpoints are valid; the T2-001 status correction is the candidate's most important finding and is confirmed by primary evidence. Confidence grades re-checked: everything Laumonier/Bloomberg-based stays reported; only the Richborough status correction (operator release) and the McKay LD4-FR2 arc (operator release) are confirmed. All power_mw null; no unsourced capacities or addresses remain; all quotes under 25 words.
results.research_notes: SEARCHED: Laumonier 'HFT in my backyard' I/II/V/last-tower (fetched), 'HFT in the Banana Land' + part 6 (fetched), Visionscarto 'HFT in the Jungle' (fetched, English version), Bloomberg NATO-towers story (original paywalled; fetched Traders Magazine syndication), Vigilant Richborough no-appeal release (PR Newswire, fetched), McKay Brothers LD4-FR2 launch release (operator-site copy 404s; PR Newswire copy fetched), Richborough approval-status searches. KEY FINDINGS BEYOND NEW PINS: (1) STATUS CORRECTION — existing T2-001 'Richborough mast (Vigilant/DRW)' was never built: Dover DC refused both the Vigilant 322m and NLN 305m applications in Jan/Feb 2017 and Vigilant confirmed no appeal; pin must be re-labeled refused-proposal/historical. (2) Houtem (T2-002) enriched with Bloomberg-syndicated purchase details (Toren Navo Aansluiting Ltd, ~EUR 5m, 243m); sources disagree Jan vs Dec 2013 on purchase date. NOT FOUND IN PUBLIC RECORD: any 'Ghislenghien' HFT mast (candidate hint) — nothing in Laumonier or elsewhere; the documented mast in that area is Flobecq. Also not obtained: direct ANFR Cartoradio records (interactive map, not fetchable — visionscarto documents Cartoradio-derived findings; a data.gouv.fr ANFR bulk-data pass would be the follow-up for French-sourced original work), Ofcom fixed-links register extracts, Belgian cadastreantenne.issep.be dossiers (linked by Laumonier part II but not fetched), exact structures for Ramsgate/Liège/Wavre/Hannut/Sint-Pieters-Leeuw (kept city_level; never fake precision), and Westrozebeke (tower destroyed; DCS site returned to Belgium 2006 — not mappable). Egem/Norkring tower omitted as a record: operator presence only speculative in source ('Vigilant really loves Norkring'). German hops (Simmerath, Feldberg/Weibern) are documented in part V but out of this candidate's FR/BE scope — worth a separate candidate. All new pins are 'reported' (Laumonier = credible specialist press with original fieldwork); only the Richborough status correction is 'confirmed' (operator's own release) and the LD4-FR2 McKay arc 'confirmed' (operator release). Current (2026) tenancy of dishes documented 2014-2017 is unverified — flagged in each capacity_note.

results.research_notes: CORRIDOR NOT FOUND — and the public record explains why the premise is wrong. Searches run (EN): 'Tokyo Osaka microwave HFT/TSE OSE arbitrage', '"Tokyo-Osaka" microwave wireless link traders 2013-2015', 'McKay Brothers Tokyo Osaka', 'Sniper in Mahwah Japan', 'J-GATE colocation data center location/history'; (JA): 東京 大阪 マイクロ波 超高速取引, J-GATE データセンター 所在地, アット東京 関西中央センター JPX. No spectrum-license, press, or academic evidence of any Tokyo-Osaka microwave route for trading exists; the IMC 2020 academic survey of the world's HFT wireless networks (bdebopam.github.io/papers/imc2020-hft.pdf, fetched) covers no Japan corridor. Structural reason: OSE's derivatives matching engine has been physically in TOKYO since J-GATE launched Feb 2011 (OSE used KVH Proximity Hosting via KVH's Tokyo Data Center per 2010 KVH/OSE announcement — quoted in MarketsWiki's Osaka Exchange entry, used only as a pointer; original COMTEX/BusinessWire PRs were unfetchable). Since July 19, 2016 J-GATE sits in the same JPX Primary Site co-location as arrowhead (JPX connectivity page, fetched via curl — jpx.co.jp 403s WebFetch), so TSE-OSE 'arbitrage' is an intra-data-center race measured in microseconds, not a 400 km corridor. Do NOT create an Osaka execution pin for OSE matching — that would map non-reality. What Osaka actually hosts (returned as new record): AT TOKYO KC1, the designated JPX Kansai Proximity Area with AP4, and JPX's Kansai secondary (BCP) DC which took the J-GATE3.0 backup on 2021-09-21 — disaster recovery, not latency arb. Documented wireless involving Japan runs TOKYO-SINGAPORE, not Tokyo-Osaka: McKay Brothers International live Nov 2016, '@Tokyo (CC2)' to SGX, <63 ms RTT hybrid microwave+fiber, distributing OSE futures data to SGX in <31.5 ms one-way (PRNewswire, both fetched) — the 'Osaka' in that headline is the exchange, whose data is picked up in Tokyo. Also documented: Go West Chicago-Tokyo consortium (Bloomberg via spokesman.com, 2016) and McKay/Go West Chicago-Tokyo partnership (Dec 2025, mckay-brothers.com/media) — suggest these as separate candidates (tokyo-singapore, chicago-tokyo) with an SGX/Singapore endpoint record before drawing arcs; no arc returned here since no documented Tokyo-Osaka route exists and Singapore/Chicago endpoints are out of this candidate's scope. NOT findable in the public record: street addresses of the JPX Primary Site (@Tokyo CC2 — baxtel: 'Full Address is Not Disclosed') and of AT TOKYO KC1 ('central Osaka' only); any MIC spectrum-license trail for trading microwave in Japan (MIC 無線局 search not queryable via web search); pre-2011 OSE hosting details (only a local-heritage page mentioning an OSE computing center in Suita, Osaka — not load-bearing, omitted).

results.reasoning: Adversarial verification of candidate "shortwave-counterparts". I fetched all 11 evidence URLs; every one is live and supports its core claim, including the FCC order FCC-25-88A1.pdf which I downloaded and read in full text (confirms Toggle/WI2XAJ Nov 6 2015 grant, CIS/WN2XCR, Skycast/WI2XER + the North America-to-Europe Wideband HF Program exhibit title, Rockland/WK2XJK, and Tower Research Capital as parent of M-Wave/ABC/Rockland; it contains NO site locations, consistent with how it was used). Raw-page greps confirmed the Elburn and Wanatah coordinates are stated verbatim in Part II and the Riverhead coordinates appear in Part III's embedded map link. All records survive, all at "reported" (locations rest on specialist-blog fieldwork and Bloomberg/IEEE press; FCC primary docs confirm identities/purposes but never locations). Changes: (1) stripped unverified specifics — Shortwave Modernization Coalition memberships for Toggle and CIS, Elburn "58-acre field", WIPE "10 kW DRM"/"~52 deg heading", "FCC warned" framing (drmna attributes the argument to objectors, not the FCC), Kane County petition number AF-2017-0355/Perry Road (not in Part III; replaced with the verified June 17 2017 zoning filing), and the unfetched Anchorage-FCC and WatersTechnology mentions in arc notes. (2) Fixed a non-verbatim coordinate "quote" for Elburn and corrected the Wanatah firm-identification note attribution to SIM/Laumonier (the bracketed annotation is signed "– SIM", not Van Valzah). (3) Downgraded the Wanatah->CME arc to inferred: Part II verifiably says the FCC-licensed hops stop at Oak Forest IL and the CME last mile is Van Valzah's explicit "hunch". (4) Downgraded Riverhead coord_precision exact->approximate (map-link centroid of a 26-acre site, not a stated point coordinate). (5) Upgraded the Raft Chicago-Tokyo arc to confirmed: the operator's own page (fetched, raw-verified) marks the route "In Production" sub-42ms — same standard as the other two Raft arcs. (6) Strengthened the Elburn record with a newly found primary source: 10Band's 2021 FCC renewal (fcc.report) lists "E-Mail Address: engineering@newlinenet.com" and a 2020 transfer of control to the William diSomma Trust, corroborating Bloomberg's New Line (Jump/Virtu) attribution; Bloomberg itself equates its "Maple Park" project with Van Valzah's third site by crediting him for uncovering it. (7) Normalized evidence_type to what was actually fetched (regulatory_docs where FCC-25-88 anchors the record, press otherwise — no actual spectrum licenses were fetched for the location claims). European receive counterparts remain absent from the public record (FCC confidentiality upheld Dec 2025), so no overseas transmitter/receiver pins — absence is the finding, correctly stated by the researcher.
results.research_notes: SEARCHED: Van Valzah's Shortwave Trading Parts I-IV (all fetched); Bloomberg via Data Center Knowledge; IEEE Spectrum; FCC order FCC 25-88 (Dec 2025, downloaded and read — confirms licensee/callsign pairs: Toggle WI2XAJ, County Information Services WN2XCR, Skycast WI2XER, Rockland WK2XJK, M-Wave WL2XEE, Alpha Bravo WM2XZU, RCA Telecom WM2XTS, and Tower Research Capital as parent of M-Wave/Alpha Bravo/Rockland); fcc.report ELS mirrors (WH2XVO transfer to Virtu Financial Operating LLC); drmna.info; SWLing Post; Radio World (403 via direct fetch); BVARC list archive; Skywave Networks corporate site and its 2024 RICO suit vs Jump/Virtu/10Band/New Line entities (Justia/PacerMonitor blocked/paywalled); Raft Technologies operator pages. NOT FINDABLE IN PUBLIC RECORD: (1) European receive/transmit counterpart sites — Van Valzah's promised 'Part V about Europe' was never published; FCC applicants' antenna/site exhibits are redacted as confidential and the FCC upheld those redactions against Skywave's FOIA challenge in FCC 25-88, so overseas endpoints stay hidden; KC1GHT (ham blog) claims UK stations sit on 'the South West tip' of England with a microwave link to London — vague, uncorroborated, not mappable; an Ofcom FOI response (Nov 2023, 'high frequency shortwave fixed stations') is Cloudflare-blocked, but the search snippet indicates Ofcom named only the Woofferton broadcast station (Encompass), i.e., no UK HF trading stations disclosed there. A widely-indexed heise.de story about a German 'Shortwave Traders' transmitter (Daun, Eifel) is an April 1, 2018 hoax — excluded. (2) Firms behind County Information Services (Wanatah), Skycast (Riverhead), RCA Telecom, DPA Mac, and the June-2018 Maple Park licensee remain unnamed publicly; Van Valzah says he identified the CIS firm but withheld it. (3) M-Wave WL2XEE and Alpha Bravo WM2XZU (both Tower Research) station locations not established from fetched sources — no pins. (4) Part IV lists further license-placed sites (Glendale Heights IL, Orangeburg NY, Wesley Hills NY, Anchorage AK, Seattle WA) — left unmapped pending stronger per-site evidence. (5) WH2XVO (Virtu) reportedly lists Aurora/Chicago IL, Homer AK, Secaucus NJ sites per KC1GHT, but not verified from primary docs. West Chicago record is a merge onto T2-004 adding licensee, IMC link, and real evidence URLs. Skywave Networks (Chicago–London shortwave patents, opponent of the SMC) discloses no site locations on its own website.

Tested RTD better than 58.95 ms NY4 Secaucus–LD4 Slough (Hibernia Networks release, 24 Sep 2015, via cabletechnologynews.co.uk); operator states it 'owns and operates the lowest latency transatlantic cable from New York to London' (exainfra.net/ultra-low-latency). Arc endpoints functionally serve existing sites T1-003 (NY4) and T1-005 (LD4), the measured latency endpoints.

results.reasoning: All seven researcher evidence URLs were fetched and are live; six support their claims as cited. Verified facts: Halifax CLS address 132 Ketch Harbour Rd, Herring Cove (Submarine Networks); EXA Express landing points Halifax/Brean/Cork, RFS 2015, owner EXA Infrastructure, 4,600 km, SubCom (TeleGeography landing-point and cable API records); route Halifax–Brean Sands–Slough–Cork, six pairs, >53 Tbps, Microsoft/Zayo customers (Lightwave); tested RTD <58.95 ms LD4 Slough–NY4 Secaucus (2015 press-release copy); Brean, Somerset station beside a caravan park, GTT 2017 acquisition (The Register fieldwork). Changes made: (1) stripped the unverified marketing quote 'specifically designed for the financial community' — the release actually says 'various segments including financial markets'; (2) stripped the unverified '58.55 ms' current-latency figure — exainfra.net carries no latency number; (3) removed the unverifiable TeleGeography coordinate claim from Halifax coord_basis (the API JSON contains no coordinates); (4) downgraded Brean coord_precision from approximate to city_level (village-centroid placement, no public address, location deliberately withheld); (5) replaced the arc evidence_url — exainfra.net verifies operator and medium but not the Halifax–Brean route — with the TeleGeography cable record, which verifies route + current operator + fiber medium in one URL; (6) downgraded the arc from confirmed to reported, since the route/landing points rest on industry-database and trade-press sources, not primary operator documents. Both records were already correctly graded reported and survive with these edits; geography checks pass (44.568,-63.561 sits in Herring Cove; 51.29,-3.012 sits in Brean). power_mw correctly null in both. Verdict: promote_with_changes.
results.research_notes: Searched: 'Hibernia Express Brean Somerset landing', 'Hibernia Express Halifax 58.95', 'Hibernia Express Herring Cove landing station', 'EXA Infrastructure Express lowest latency'. Fetched and verified: The Register 2019 Brean fieldwork piece; TeleGeography submarinecablemap API (cable exa-express: 4,600 km, RFS Sept 2015, owner EXA Infrastructure, supplier SubCom; landing points Halifax NS / Brean UK / Cork IE; geo API gives Halifax point at 44.6458,-63.5740 which is downtown Halifax, i.e. symbolic); Submarine Networks Halifax CLS page (street address 132 Ketch Harbour Rd, Herring Cove NS; CLS built by Hibernia Networks, lands Hibernia Atlantic and Hibernia Express, later GTT); Lightwave RFS article (Halifax–Slough via Brean Sands landing, 59 ms NY4–LD4, 53 Tbps, Microsoft and Zayo customers); Hibernia Networks press-release text via cabletechnologynews.co.uk ('better than 58.95ms', LD4 Slough–NY4 Secaucus, 24 Sep 2015); exainfra.net ultra-low-latency page (operator claim of lowest-latency NY–London cable, financial-exchange focus). NOT findable in the public record: original Business Wire URL timed out (used verbatim reproduction instead); exact parcel/coordinates of the Brean station (The Register deliberately withholds; internetmaps.co.uk is offline and web.archive.org is unfetchable in this environment); no named quant/HFT trading-firm customers of the cable — only 'financial community' marketing plus Microsoft/Zayo; no MW power figures for either CLS. Cork, Ireland branch landing exists (TeleGeography, Submarine Networks) but was omitted per instructions (two landing points + one arc). Suggested map linkage: the arc endpoints functionally serve existing sites T1-003 (NY4) and T1-005 (LD4), which are the measured latency endpoints.

results.reasoning: All six evidence URLs were fetched and every one is live and supports the claim it was cited for, so the record and all three arcs survive. Verified directly: Quincy's own coverage page lists Aurora IL as a venue (operator docs, primary); the 2012 Quincy/PR Newswire release confirms the Aurora-Newark/Secaucus microwave route live since July 2012; the 2016 release confirms Aurora as the futures-data source and gives one-way latencies to Secaucus-NY2 (4.015 ms), Mahwah (3.986 ms) and Carteret (3.982 ms) over McKay's microwave network; DCK (citing FCC records) puts McKay affiliate Jefferson Microwave among three licensees on CyrusOne's 350-ft Aurora tower and links Jefferson to McKay; Databento documents the 2905 E. Diehl Road address and 2018 tower completion; DCK's 2017 land-war piece confirms the Pierce Broadband Oct 2015 lease, county approval for a 350-ft tower, Tyc saying it may never be built, and IMC/Tower Research minority stakes. Confidence stays confirmed because the core head-end claim rests on verified operator docs and corporate announcements, though I noted in the rationale that the CyrusOne-tower placement specifically is press-citing-FCC (ULS pages were never fetched by researcher or me). Changes made: capacity_note now uses the source-stated futures categories (Interest Rate/Equity Index/Energy/Metals/FX) instead of the researcher's CME/CBOT/NYMEX/COMEX paraphrase; the unverified Sniper-in-Mahwah 4.062 ms corroboration was stripped from the Mahwah arc note; 2016 latency figure in the Secaucus arc note is now attributed to its actual (verified) source; firms_linked attributed to DCK. Merge target T2-003 and arc endpoints T1-001/002/003 check out against data/sites_seed.csv; coordinates 41.7965/-88.2445 plausibly sit on E. Diehl Road in Aurora IL, matching the documented address, and coord_precision correctly remains approximate (address-derived, not source-stated coords). Geography, status=active (current operator page), and power_mw=null all pass; no quotes exceed 25 words.
results.research_notes: Searched: Quincy Data/McKay Brothers Aurora head-end; McKay/Scientel/CyrusOne tower dispute (2017-2019); Quincy Data service docs and PR releases; FCC ULS for McKay shells; CME wireless colocation offering; Sniper in Mahwah Aurora coverage. FINDINGS: The Quincy Data Aurora head-end is the SAME site as existing T2-003 (CyrusOne Aurora campus, 2905 E. Diehl Road) — returned as a merge, not a new pin. Since 2018 the physical antenna point is CyrusOne's 350-ft tower on that campus; DCK (citing FCC records) lists Jefferson Microwave LLC (McKay), New Line Networks LLC (Jump/Virtu) and Webline Holdings LLC as licensed operators on it — useful corroboration for other candidates too. NOT FINDABLE in the public record: (1) a street address for the parcel McKay leased via Pierce Broadband LLC in Oct 2015 ('diagonal to the CME data center', DuPage County records per DCK) — county approval for a 350-ft tower existed but co-founder Tyc said it might never be built, so no separate pin; (2) FCC ULS license pages themselves (wireless2.fcc.gov timed out repeatedly) — Jefferson Microwave call signs WQUR984/WQRN948/WQVG418 surfaced in search results but pages were not fetched, so the tower-license claim rests on DCK press citing FCC records; (3) the exact location of McKay's pre-2018 original Aurora tower (only 'its current location' relative wording in press); (4) Crain's 2017 article text (paywalled). Scientel's 195-ft tower across the street is documented but excluded — it is a network integrator's tower, not a documented quant/market-data head-end. Quincy also 'sources Equity Index and Energy futures in Chicago' (likely legacy Cermak area) but no address is given publicly — no pin. CME Group's own vendor page for Quincy Data (cmegroup.com) timed out; not cited.

checked.comment: Abstract page verified: 'For DL training, we deployed the Fire-Flyer 2 with 10,000 PCIe A100 GPUs'. 52 authors, contact Wenfeng Liang. No location, no MW figure on the abs page (those are in the PDF body).

PDF downloaded and text-extracted locally (WebFetch could not read the binary). Verbatim-verified: 'the total energy consumption of the Fire-Flyer 2 AI-HPC does not exceed 4 MW, approximately just over 3 MW'; 'approximately 1,250 GPU compute nodes and nearly 200 storage servers'; construction in 2021; affiliation DeepSeek-AI (Beijing contact address); acknowledgment thanks 'colleagues at DeepSeek-AI and High-Flyer Quant'. No physical cluster location named anywhere in the paper. No kW figure for Fire-Flyer 1 — researcher's '400 kW' is unsupported by any evidence URL.

Quotes verify (second cluster ~10,000 A100s, 1bn yuan, completed a year after 2020; first cluster 1,100 A100s, 200m yuan, 2020; DeepSeek as 'independent research group envisaged by High-Flyer'; 'Hangzhou Huanfang Technology Ltd Co.'; clusters built before the September 2022 A100 export ban). BUT the publisher is Reuters (Eduardo Baptista, Jan 29 2025), NOT Financial Times as the researcher claimed — attribution corrected. Article does not name the clusters' physical location.
Official operator site. Hangzhou HQ address verified: 杭州市拱墅区环城北路 169 号 汇金国际大厦 A 座 14 层. Fire-Flyer 2 (萤火二号) platform documented. No physical cluster location stated.
36kr/暗涌Waves profile verified. Verbatim: 「萤火二号」的投入增加到10亿元，搭载了约1万张英伟达A100显卡; Fire-Flyer 1 近2亿元 / 1100块GPU; early GPUs 托管在IDC before self-building. No location named.

Pointer only per project rules (never evidence). Consistent with Fire-Flyer 2 in operation as of 2025; adversarial search found NO source that names the cluster's physical location, so the researcher's absence-finding stands.

records.capacity_note: Fire-Flyer 2 (built 2021): ~10,000 PCIe A100 GPUs, ~1,250 GPU compute nodes, ~200 storage servers; RMB 1bn cost (Reuters, 36kr); operator's SC24 paper states total draw 'approximately just over 3 MW' (<4 MW). Predecessor Fire-Flyer 1 (operational 2020, historical): 1,100 A100s, ~RMB 200m. Both clusters pre-date the September 2022 US A100 export ban (Reuters). Early GPUs were hosted in third-party IDCs before High-Flyer self-built (36kr). Cluster trained DeepSeek's early models.

records.confidence_rationale: Existence, operator, scale (10k PCIe A100s, ~1,250 nodes), 2021 build, RMB 1bn cost and ~3 MW draw are verified in primary operator-authored sources (DeepSeek-AI/High-Flyer SC24 paper, official website), corroborated by Reuters and 36kr — all five URLs fetched and quote-verified. The LOCATION is not public: no source names any city or facility for the cluster; coord_precision is symbolic at the operator's documented Hangzhou HQ and the map label must say so. (Fixed from researcher draft: the Yahoo-syndicated article is Reuters, not FT.)

results.reasoning: All five evidence URLs were fetched and verified live and supporting. The arXiv PDF (WebFetch returned raw binary) was downloaded and text-extracted locally: the MW quote ('does not exceed 4 MW, approximately just over 3 MW'), the 10,000 PCIe A100 deployment, ~1,250 compute nodes, ~200 storage servers, construction in 2021, and DeepSeek-AI/High-Flyer Quant authorship are all verbatim in the paper, which names no physical location. Corrections applied: (1) publisher misattribution — the Yahoo Finance article is a Reuters piece by Eduardo Baptista (2025-01-29), not FT; quotes verify, attribution fixed. (2) Stripped Fire-Flyer 1 '400 kW' — no surviving URL backs it (researcher attributed it to uncited Sohu/Zhihu coverage). (3) Export-controls date fixed to September 2022 per Reuters ('banned from export to China in September 2022... built and put into operation long before'). (4) Geography: lat/lon sit in Hangzhou, but no source places the CLUSTER in Hangzhou — only the operator HQ is documented there — so coord_precision downgraded from city_level to symbolic with an explicit coord_basis. (5) Corrected the arxiv/abs quote to text actually on the abstract page. Retained: power_mw=3 (source-stated approximation in operator paper), status=active (paper describes ongoing operation supporting DeepSeek model training; adversarial search found no decommissioning report), confidence=confirmed (strongest verified sources are primary operator docs, corroborated by Reuters and 36kr). An independent search for the cluster's physical location (English + Chinese) found nothing — the researcher's absence-is-the-finding conclusion stands, consistent with project rule 4. No arcs (research tier). Verdict: promote_with_changes.
results.research_notes: SEARCHED: English (High-Flyer Fire-Flyer 10,000 A100 location; DeepSeek GPU cluster data center province) and Chinese (幻方量化 萤火二号 数据中心 位置; 萤火 机房 在哪 托管 IDC; "萤火二号" 位于/坐落/部署在; 萤火一号 篮球场; 汇金国际大厦). FETCHED & VERIFIED: high-flyer.cn homepage + /history/ (specs, Hangzhou/Shanghai office addresses; no cluster location), FT-via-Yahoo profile (verbatim cluster cost/date quotes re-verified), arXiv 2408.14158 Fire-Flyer AI-HPC paper (PDF downloaded, text extracted with pypdf — MW and GPU-count quotes verified verbatim; no location given), 36kr/暗涌 deep profile (states early clusters were 托管在IDC/hosted in third-party IDC before self-building, but names no place). NOT FINDABLE IN PUBLIC RECORD: the physical data-center location (city, province, or facility) of Fire-Flyer 1 or 2 — no planning filing, press report, or operator statement names it; this absence is the finding, and the pin is city-level at Hangzhou (operator HQ) per project convention. REJECTED SOURCES: promptlayer.com SEO blog claims (Hohhot centers, Hainan underwater DC) are unsourced/conflated with unrelated third-party facilities; Wikipedia used only as pointer. Fire-Flyer 1 details (1,100 GPUs, ~RMB 200m/近2亿元, 400 kW, basketball-court footprint, live March 2020) appear in Sohu/zhihu coverage and the 36kr profile — folded into capacity_note as historical rather than a second pin at identical unknown coordinates. Note discrepancy: official history page frames 萤火一号 earlier (2016-2018 development) vs press (operational 2020); FT dates used. No Tier-2 arcs applicable (research-tier site).

One misattributed quote found and fixed: the researcher's 'CoreWeave Cloud is tailored to the specific requirements...' sentence does not appear in the cited DCD article (likely from the case study); replaced with a verified verbatim sentence from that article. power_mw null on both (no source-stated MW for existing sites; the 100-200MW figure is for a planned, unlocated facility and remains unmapped). results.reasoning: Adversarial check of both records. Every evidence URL was fetched and verified: four gresearch.com/scottbrownrigg pages via WebFetch (all quotes verbatim, dates match), the Wayback job-ad capture and eFinancialCareers via curl (WebFetch blocks archive.org and gets 405 from eFC, but curl retrieves both, so they count as accessible), and the Texas TDLR record via WebFetch. Nothing was dead or misattributed; every quote is under 25 words and appears verbatim on its page. Grading holds at 'confirmed' for both records because the core location claims rest on verified corporate announcements plus a contractor project page and a state registry record — the job-ad material only feeds the capacity notes, where its recruitment-page provenance is stated. Changes made: (1) the Dallas capacity note quoted an HPC Network Engineer ad with no URL in the evidence list — I located a genuine Wayback capture via the CDX index (not reconstructed), verified the quotes ('large multi-vendor, multi-protocol data centers and high-performance compute networks', Ethernet/InfiniBand, Dallas hub), and added it as evidence, also correcting the quote to the ad's US spelling 'data centers'; (2) removed the unverified '15-story, 352,000 sq ft / building's own site' claim from the Dallas rationale (no URL supplied); (3) softened 'fleet of thousands of servers' to the Armada blog's stated aim; (4) clarified in the London rationale that the Soho Place announcement calls it the 'fourth London office' and the HQ label comes from the contractor page; (5) trimmed the Dallas address parenthetical to note the TDLR record anchors the building, not G-Research's suite. Geography is sound: 51.5162,-0.1301 sits at Tottenham Court Road/1 Soho Place; 32.7876,-96.809 sits in Victory Park by the American Airlines Center — both correctly flagged approximate pending geocode. power_mw is null in both (no source-stated figure exists; 'multiple megawatts' stays as a quoted characterisation only). No arcs claimed, correctly — no point-to-point link evidence exists. The researcher's absence findings (no named colo partner, GPU count, or MW total anywhere public; anonymized WWT case study rightly excluded) match my checks. Verdict: promote_with_changes.

FOUND (primary, fetched): (1) archived Data Centre Engineer job ad (Wayback 2025-03-14) stating verbatim 'The Data Centre Team manages a global estate of data centres with multiple megawatts of capacity', 'we have data centres in multiple regions', and 'Liaising with third party data centre, colocation and service providers' — i.e. a multi-region colo estate; (2) archived HPC Network Engineer ad (Wayback 2025-09-26) on 'large multi-vendor, multi-protocol data centre and high-performance compute networks' incl. InfiniBand, based in the Dallas hub; (3) Armada engineering blog (2020): 'large, fixed-pool of on-prem compute', fleet of thousands of servers; (4) corporate announcements for 1 Soho Place London HQ (2023) and One Victory Commons Dallas hub (2024). NOT FINDABLE in the public record: any named data-centre location or colo partner, any GPU count, any total MW figure, any DCD/press coverage of their facilities (contrast XTX Kajaani). A WWT case study 'Quant Hedge Fund Builds Software-defined GPU Data Center' is anonymized and cannot be attributed — excluded. Recruitment pages say only 'near-infinite compute'. Conclusion: G-Research's compute footprint is real, multi-megawatt and multi-region per its own hiring materials, but its locations are deliberately undisclosed — map the two documented corporate sites and state the absence. results.reasoning: All 8 evidence URLs fetched live and support their claims, with three corrections. (1) XTX linkage: The Currency confirms an XTX geothermal supercomputer in Iceland but names no host; Keldan confirms only an XTX Iceland branch; my independent searches (FT-profile coverage via Techmeme, DC directories) likewise never name Verne, and Reykjanesbær hosts rival operators (atNorth ICE02, Borealis). Seed T3-002's 'Verne Global campus' assertion for XTX is not in the public record, so XTX is stripped from firms_linked and recorded as a negative finding — stronger than the researcher's 'inferred-at-best' suggestion. (2) Unsupported specifics stripped: tenants DeepL/BMW/Volkswagen/ThreatMetrix/Datto/Earlham/Nebius (the archived customer page and Nebius claim were not in the evidence set), the 'Iceland's main telecom interconnect hub / subsea connectivity' claim (verne.co fetch shows no such text), and the Efstaleiti 5 registered-office detail (not visible on the fetched Keldan page). (3) Geography verified: HOSTKEY address is verbatim; OSM way 35973808 ('Verne Global Datacenter', Valhallarbraut 869) independently fetched — researcher's coords are its centroid, so exact precision stands and the seed's coords are confirmed wrong. Campus record stays 'confirmed' on operator docs (verne.co) corroborated by DCD and Icelandic press; power_mw=140 is source-stated. No arcs claimed. Verdict: promote_with_changes.
results.research_notes: Searched (EN + Icelandic): 'Verne Global Keflavik NATO XTX', '"XTX Markets" "Verne Global"', 'XTX Keflavik supercomputer', 'XTX gagnaver Ísland Reykjanesbær', site-restricted searches of visir.is/mbl.is/ruv.is/vb.is, 'XTX atNorth/Advania', eFinancialCareers, and job boards. Fetched: verne.co/iceland (operator, 140+MW), DCD 10MW-expansion and Nscale-15MW articles, braggi.is 120MW Asbru expansion, Visir/Innherji on the Digital 9 acquisition, HOSTKEY address page, The Currency Gerko profile, DCD XTX-Kajaani article, XTX greenhouse job board (no Iceland roles), Keldan registry entry. Also checked Wayback CDX for any verneglobal.com/verne.co URL containing 'xtx' (none) and the archived 2019 customer-success page (named customers: ThreatMetrix, DeepL, RX Networks, Datto, Earlham Institute — no XTX). KEY NEGATIVE FINDING: nothing in the public record names Verne as the host of XTX's Iceland supercomputer. XTX-in-Iceland is well documented (FT profile via Techmeme headline; The Currency; registered branch XTX Markets Limited útibú á Íslandi, kennitala 5106190800, Efstaleiti 5 Reykjavík), and DCD quotes XTX saying its 'need for compute has outgrown available leasing options' (implying leased third-party space), but the lessor is never named. Caution for the essay: Asbru/Keflavik hosts several operators — atNorth's ICE02 mega-site is also on the former base area, and BNP Paribas's Iceland HPC is at atNorth, NOT Verne — so 'former NATO base near Keflavik' reporting does not by itself identify Verne. Recommendation: merge this campus record into T3-002, keep the campus pin as confirmed (Verne campus), downgrade/annotate the XTX linkage as inferred-at-best per project rule 4 (absence is the finding), and adopt corrected coordinates 63.9778, -22.5758.

critic:
**1. Suspicious rejections**
- `tokyo-osaka-microwave` is the only rejection and it is not credible as "unsourceable." McKay Brothers publicly announced/markets a Tokyo–Osaka wireless service (press releases + their published latency tables between JPX colo sites), and Japan's MIC radio-license register (無線局等情報検索) is the FCC-ULS equivalent for path-level evidence. Likely the sweep searched English press only. Re-run against: McKay Brothers Japan announcements, MIC license search, JPX arrowhead/J-GATE colo docs.

**2. Over-graded records**
- Jane Street on CoreWeave `[confirmed/symbolic]` — relationship is press-reported, not vendor/primary disclosure → `reported`.
- Jump Trading Vera Rubin NVL72 cluster `[confirmed/symbolic]` — unless it comes from an NVIDIA/Jump primary announcement, this is press/keynote hearsay → `reported`.
- HRT at Lefdal Mine `[confirmed/approximate]` — if the source is DCD/trade-press rather than a Lefdal or HRT announcement naming HRT, this is `reported`.
- Maple Park silo shortwave `[reported/city_level]` — "licensee undisclosed" means the HFT attribution is triangulated → `inferred`.
- Batavia WPBC (Parable Broadcasting) `[reported/city_level]` — the trading link is Van Valzah-style inference from a broadcast license → `inferred`.
- SGX Keppel DC `[reported/exact]` — exact coords on a reported source violates rule 3 discipline; verify address before `exact`, else `approximate`.
- Chicago–NJ chain "McKay Aurora head-end" `[confirmed/city_level]` — precision downgrade vs existing T2-003 (`approximate`); shouldn't ship a worse-precision duplicate.

**3. Duplicates / merges with T1-001..T3-004**
- `verne-global-keflavik` ≡ T3-002 (XTX at Verne Global Keflavik) — merge, keep the new `exact` coords.
- Aurora head-end triple: `quincy-data-aurora` record + `chicago-nj-chain` McKay record ≡ T2-003 — one site, three records; merge (Quincy Data is McKay's data arm).
- `shortwave-counterparts` West Chicago (Toggle/IMC) ≡ T2-004.
- `london-frankfurt-hops` Richborough ≡ T2-001; Houtem ≡ T2-002.
- Swingate and Tour de/du Reuze appear in BOTH `channel-swingate-dunkirk` and `london-frankfurt-hops` (also spelling drift: "de Reuze" vs "du Reuze") — dedupe to one record each.
- `fx-ebs-lseg-trisite` NY4/NY5 ≡ T1-003 and LD4 ≡ T1-005 — should be firms_linked/capacity_note annotations on existing pins, not new pins.
- Fire-Flyer 2 appears in both `high-flyer-fire-flyer` and `ubiquant-chinese-quants` — keep one.
- Jump/World Class Wireless Aurora parcel appears in both `jump-hpc` and `chicago-nj-chain` ("New Line Networks Aurora antenna site" — WCW is Jump's license shell; same 31-acre lot) — merge.
- XTX Kajaani Building 2 should cross-reference/nest under T3-001 (same Renforsin Ranta campus), not float free.
- CyrusOne Aurora tower shares coords with T1-004 — legitimate distinct asset, but flag so the map doesn't render as a stray duplicate.

**4. Missing items implied by the candidate list**
- `anova-nj-triangle`: 3 arcs, 0 records — arcs have no candidate-owned endpoints; confirm all three vertices resolve to T1-001/T1-002/T1-003 site_ids, otherwise arcs are dangling. If Anova used non-exchange rooftop relays, those records are missing.
- No Osaka site anywhere: rejecting `tokyo-osaka-microwave` leaves the JPX Osaka/J-GATE colo endpoint entirely absent — even a fixed T1 pin is missing, independent of the microwave route.
- `shortwave-counterparts`: 8 arcs but all surviving records are US-side; zero European/receive counterpart stations (the candidate name literally promises counterparts) — transatlantic arcs have no far ends.
- `hibernia-express`: only the Halifax–Brean ocean arc; missing terrestrial backhaul arcs to T1-003 (Secaucus) and T1-005 (Slough), which is the entire latency-trading relevance of the cable.
- `chicago-nj-chain`: named "chain" but no mid-path relay towers survived (Illinois/Indiana/Ohio/Pennsylvania hops) — arcs jump end-to-end, contradicting the candidate's premise.
- `london-frankfurt-hops`: Belgian hops end at Liège; no German-side relays before Frankfurt (FR2/T1-006) — gap in the corridor.
- `krx-busan`: two records that are literally a proximity pair (KRX engine + Koscom/Colt proximity DC) yet 0 arcs — the Busan pair and/or Seoul–Busan arc is implied and missing.
